DSSSB Results | Cutoff | Marks | Score Card 2026

DSSSB 2026 Result : Short Details of Notification

OnlyJobs® | WWW.OnlyJobs

Exam Conducted By

  • Delhi Subordinate Service Selection Board DSSSB Are Conducted the Various Recruitment Exam in Every Year.

Application Fee

  • No Application Fee to Check the DSSSB Latest Results, Cutoff, Marks and Score Card.

OnlyJobs® : DSSSB Result 2026 How to Check

  • DSSSB (Delhi Subordinate Services Selection Board) exam results can be checked easily through OnlyJobs®. Candidates searching for OnlyJobs DSSSB Result 2026 can follow the methods below to check their result, marks, and cutoff details.
  • There are two main methods to check DSSSB results – first through PDF and second by logging into the DSSSB online portal.
  • First : DSSSB Result PDF Method
    In many cases, DSSSB releases the result in PDF format. Candidates need to download the PDF and search their roll number using the Ctrl + F option. If the roll number is found in the list, the candidate is considered qualified.
  • Second : DSSSB Online Portal Login Method
    Some results are available through the DSSSB online portal where candidates can check their scorecard, marks, and detailed result.
  • To login, candidates need to enter:
  • Date of Birth
  • Class 10 Roll Number
  • Passing Year
  • Password created during the application process
  • After logging in, click on the “View Result” tab to check your result and marks.
For the latest updates on DSSSB Result, Admit Card, Answer Key, and Cutoff, always visit OnlyJobs, the official website for OnlyJobs® in India (Since 2012).

DSSSB Important Note (Qualified Candidate)

  • Candidates who qualify in the DSSSB examination may be required to fill the E-Dossier Form. Complete details will be available in the official result or cutoff notice.

Enrolled Candidate Can Download the Delhi DSSSB Latest Result 2026.

  • For Subject Wise Eligibility Details Read the Notification.

How to Fill UPPSC Assistant Teacher LT Grade Recruitment Online Form 2025

  • Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission UPPSC Call For New Application for UPPSC Uttar Pradesh Assistant Teacher LT Grade Post Recruitment Examination 2025 Recruitment Candidate Can Apply Between 28/07/2025 to 28/08/2025
  • It is mandatory to have OTR i.e. One Time Registration for UPPSC Recruitment.
  • Pay attention, the registration number is received only after 72 hours of OTR registration - that's why OTR should be done first and only then the application will be made.
  • Candidate Read the Notification Before Apply the Recruitment Application Form in Uttar Pradesh UPPSC Latest Uttar Pradesh LT Grade Assistant Teacher Exam 2025-2026- UP Government Jobs 2025.
  • Kindly Check and Collect the All Document - Eligibility, ID Proof, Address Details, Basic Details.
  • Kindly Ready Scan Document Related to Recruitment Form - Photo, Sign, ID Proof, Etc.
  • Before Submit the Application Form Must Check the Preview and All Column Carefully.
  • If Candidate Required to Paying the Application Fee Must Submit. If You have Not the Required Application Fees Your Form is Not Completed.
  • Take A Print Out of Final Submitted Form.

Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)

CBSE Board Class Xth and Class XIIth Result 2026

CBSE Results 2026 | Short Details of Notification

OnlyJobs® Official WWW.OnlyJobs

Important Dates

  • Class 10th High School Exam Between : 17/02/2026 to 11/03/2026
  • Class 12th Intermediate Exam Between : 17/02/2026 to 10/04/2026
  • Digilocker Account Activate Through Security Pin :14/04/2026
  • Class 10th Result Declared : 15/04/2026
  • Class 10th Second Board Exam Date : 15-21 May 2026
  • Class 12th Result Declared : 13 May 2026

Application Fee

  • No Application Fee for the Checking CBSE Board Term II Class X & XII Exam Result & Activate Your DIGI Locker Account Through Security Pin.
 

OnlyJobs® :  How to Activate DIGILOCKER Account Through School Security Pin 2026

  • CBSE has issued a notice on 14 April 2026, in which it has been said that the annual board examination year 2026 of class 10 and 12 results / certificates and migration will be through Digilocker.
  • All CBSE Board Class 10 and 12 students who have also appeared in the exam will have to create their own account in Digilocker.
  • CBSE Board students can create their account in Digilocker by following some simple steps - which CBSE has also mentioned in its notice.
  • Once the account is created in Digilocker, candidates can also download their CBSE Board 2026 result, marksheet, certificate and migration online after the issue.
  • Step by Step to Activate CBSE Student to Activate Digilocker Account.
  • Step 1 : The student will get the link to do DigiLocker Account Confirmation in the Some Useful Important Links section at the bottom of this page.
  • Step 2 :To confirm DigiLocker Account, the student has to first select his class such as X or XII.
  • Step 3 : After that the student has to enter the code of his school. If the school code is not known, then they can see it in their admit card.
  • Step 4 : After that the student has to enter his/her board exam roll number.
  • Step 5 : After that Enter 6 Digit PIN provided by your school.
  • Step 6 : After that the candidate has to enter his mobile number.
  • Step 7 : An OTP will be received on the mobile number given by the student, he will have to enter it.
  • Step 8 : DigiLocker Account Confirmation process will be completed.
  • Now waiting for the candidate, whenever the CBSE Board will release the result of 2026 examination, candidates can also check their result through Digilocker as well as download the passing certificate / mark sheet and migration.

How to Check CBSE Board 10th & 12th Results 2026

  • CBSE Board Exam 2026 result can be released anytime in the month of April / May 2026.
  • After the release of CBSE result of board exam, candidates can check their result through two means.
  • After the release of CBSE result, candidates can check their class 10 and 12 result through CBSEResult and Digilocker whose direct link will be found in OnlyJobs Some Useful Important Link of this page.
  • The student must have his/her roll number to view the CBSE Result 2026.
  • All the registered students of CBSE Exam 2026 must activate their Digilocker account.

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).

  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post

  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

Important Links for SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  • SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Download: ssc.gov.in (login to Result/Marks)
  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

  • General : 1700/-
  • EWS / OBC : 1600/-
  • SC / ST / PH : 1000/-
  • Out Side India : 9500/-
  • Pay the Examination Fee Through E Challan Offline Mode or Pay the Exam Fee Through Online Debit Card, Credit Card or Net Banking Fee Mode

NEET UG Eligibility 2026

  • Passed 10+2 Intermediate Exam with Physics, Chemistry, Biology PCB Group of Subjects in Any Recognized Board in India.
  • For More Details Kindly See the Full Information Brochure or Read the Code Wise Eligibility Details.

NEET UG 2026 Age Limit

  • Minimum Age : 17 Years
  • Maximum Age : No Limit
  • born on or before 31.12.2009
  • Age Relaxation Extra as per Rules.
 

Eligibility Code for NTA NEET 2026 UG

  • NEET UG Code 01: A candidate who is appearing in the qualifying examination, i.e., 12 Standard in 2025 whose result is awaited may apply and take up the said test but he/she shall not be eligible for admission to the MBBS or BDS, if he/she does not pass the qualifying examination with the required pass percentage of marks at the time of first round of Counseling.
  • NEET UG Code 02 : The Higher/Senior Secondary Examination or the Indian School Certificate Examination which is equivalent to 10+2 Higher/Senior Secondary Examination after a period of 12 years study, the last two years of such study comprising of Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Bio-technology (which shall include practical tests in these subjects) and Mathematics or any other elective subject with English at a level not less than the core course for English as prescribed by the National Council of Educational Research and Training after introduction of the 10+2+3 educational structure as recommended by the National Committee on Education.
  • NEET UG Code 03 : The Intermediate/Pre-degree Examination in Science of an Indian University/Board or other recognized examining body with Physics, Chemistry, Biology /Bio-technology (which shall include practical test in these subjects) and also English as a compulsory subject
  • NEET UG Code 04 : The Pre-professional/Pre-medical Examination with Physics ,Chemistry, Biology/Bio-technology & English after passing either the Higher Secondary Examination or the Pre-University or an equivalent examination. The Pre-professional/Pre-medical examination shall include practical test in these subjects and also English as a compulsory subject.
  • NEET UG Code 05 : The first year of the three years’ degree course of a recognized University with Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Bio-technology including practical tests in these subjects provided the examination is a University Examination and candidate has passed the earlier qualifying examination with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Bio-Technology with English at a level not less than a core course.
  • NEET UG Code 06 : B.Sc.Examination of an Indian University provided that he/she has passed the B.Sc.Examination with not less than two of the subjects Physics, Chemistry, Biology (Botany, Zoology)/Bio-technology and further that he/she has passed the earlier qualifying examination with Physics,Chemistry,Biology and English.
  • NEET UG Code 07: Any other examination which in scope and standard (Last 02 years of 10+2 Study
    comprising of Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Biotechnology; which shall include
    practical test in these subjects) is found to be equivalent to the Intermediate Science
    Examination of an Indian University/Board, taking Physics, Chemistry and
    Biology/Biotechnology including practical tests in each of these subjects and English.

Important Instruction for NEET UG

  • Aadhar Card is Compulsory in All India NTA NEET 2026 Examination
  • Instruction Of Photo  : Candidate Live Photo & Clear Passport Size and 4x6 Size Photo  (Visible Including Ears) with White
  • Background  and Also Enter Your Candidates Name and Photo Taken Date after 01/01/2026
  • For More Details Kindly  SEE NEET UG 2026 Notification

How to Download HAL Technician Admit Card 2026?

Follow these simple steps to download your HAL Technician Admit Card 2026:

  1. Step 1: Visit the official HAL website at hal-india.co.in or click the direct download link provided above.
  2. Step 2: Navigate to the "Careers" or "Recruitment" / "Admit Card" section on the homepage.
  3. Step 3: Click on the "HAL Technician Admit Card 2026" link.
  4. Step 4: Enter your credentials (Application/Registration Number, Date of Birth, etc.) and Captcha Code.
  5. Step 5: Click on "Submit" or "Download Admit Card" button.
  6. Step 6: Your HAL Technician Admit Card 2026 will appear on screen.
  7. Step 7: Download and save the PDF file on your device.
  8. Step 8: Take 2-3 printouts for exam day (keep backup copies).

Pro Tip: Check all details on your admit card immediately after download. Report any discrepancies to HAL helpdesk promptly.

Details on HAL Technician Admit Card 2026

Your HAL Technician Admit Card 2026 will contain the following information:

Candidate Information:

  • Candidate Name
  • Father's Name
  • Date of Birth
  • Category
  • Gender
  • Application/Roll Number
  • Candidate's Photograph and Signature

Exam Information:

  • Exam Name: Technician Written Test
  • Exam Date & Shift/Timing
  • Reporting Time
  • Exam Center Name and Complete Address
  • Exam Center Code

Important Instructions:

  • Documents to carry on exam day
  • Items allowed and not allowed in examination hall
  • Exam day rules

Documents Required on HAL Technician Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • HAL Technician Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport — any one)
  • Recent passport-size photographs (same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, and electronic devices
  • Calculators, books, notes
  • Bags and prohibited items

Important Instructions for HAL Technician Exam 2026

Before Exam Day:

  • Download admit card well in advance
  • Check exam center location
  • Arrange all required documents
  • Keep backup copies

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center early (45-60 minutes before reporting time)
  • Entry closes before exam starts (No late entry)
  • Follow all instructions

What If HAL Technician Admit Card Not Available?

If you’re unable to download your HAL Technician Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again.
  2. Use a different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge).
  3. Verify your application status on the official website.
  4. Contact HAL helpdesk immediately.

Important: Act quickly if facing issues. Don’t wait until exam day.

Stay Updated: Bookmark reliable sources for latest updates on HAL Technician Result 2026, answer key, and notifications.

Documents Required on MPESB ITI Training Officer Exam Day

Mandatory Documents (Originals):

  • MPESB ITI Training Officer Admit Card 2026 (printed copy with clear photograph)
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (any one of the following):
    • Aadhaar Card
    • Voter ID Card
    • Driving License
    • PAN Card
    • Passport
  • Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies - same as uploaded in application)
  • Simple pen (blue/black ballpoint)

Optional but Recommended:

Not Allowed in Examination Hall:

Important Instructions for MPESB ITI Training Officer Exam 2026

  • Download admit card 2-3 days before exam date
  • Check exam center location on Google Maps
  • Plan your route and transportation
  • Arrange all required documents in advance
  • Keep backup copies of admit card
  • Read exam instructions carefully

On Exam Day:

  • Reach exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time
  • Entry closes 30 minutes before exam starts (No late entry allowed)
  • Follow dress code if specified in admit card
  • Carry water and light snacks for outside exam hall
  • Keep calm and focused

During Exam:

  • Follow all invigilator instructions carefully
  • Fill OMR sheet with black/blue ballpoint pen only (if applicable)
  • Manage your time wisely for all sections
  • Don't panic if questions seem difficult
  • Review answers if time permits
  • Don't carry prohibited items inside exam hall

What If MPESB ITI Training Officer Admit Card Not Available?

If you're unable to download your MPESB ITI Training Officer Admit Card 2026, try these solutions:

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website to verify if your application was accepted
  4. Solution 4: Contact MPESB helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact MPESB helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

Jharkhand JHTET 2026 Exam : Eligibility Details

 

 Primary Level Class 1-5

 
  • 10+2 Intermediate Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2- year Diploma in Elementary Education (B.T.C./D.El.Ed.
  • 10+2 Intermediate Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2- year Diploma in Elementary Education (B.T.C./D.El.Ed.), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure), Regulations, 2002
  •  10+2 Intermediate Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4- year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed.)
  •  Intermediate 10+2 Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 2- year Diploma in Education (Special Education.)
  •  Bachelor Degree (or its equivalent) and passed or appearing in final year of two year Diploma in Elementary Education (B.T.C./DELED.
  • More Eligibility Details Read the Notification

 

Junior Level Class 6-8
  • Bachelor Degree (or its equivalent) and passed or appearing in final year of 2-Year Diploma in Elementary Education (B.T.C./ D.El.Ed.)
  • Bachelor Degree (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of Bachelor in Education (B.Ed/L.T./Shiksha Shastri (Regular)
  • Bachelor Degree (or its equivalent) with at least 45% marks and passed or appearing in final year of Bachelor in Education (B.Ed/L.T./Shiksha Shastri (Regular)), in accordance with the NCTE (Recognition Norms and Procedure) Regulations issued from time to time in this regard.
  • 10+2 Intermediate / Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4- year Bachelor of Elementary Education (B.El.Ed)
  • 10+2 Intermediate / Senior Secondary (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of 4- year B.A/B.Sc.Ed or B.A.Ed/B.Sc.Ed.
  • Bachelor Degree (or its equivalent) with at least 50% marks and passed or appearing in final year of B.Ed. (Special Education)
  • Master Degree with 55% Marks and 3 Year Integrated B.Ed-M.Ed Course.
  • More Eligibility Details Read the Notification

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) have officially announced the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) Recruitment 2026 with 11,127 openings. The detailed notification was published on 14 May 2026. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 30 years and hold Matriculation + ITI or Diploma/Degree in Engineering can now submit their online applications from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Railway Recruitment Boards have officially released Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) No. 01/2026 for recruitment to 11127 posts of Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) under various Railway Zones, Government of India. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination syllabus, medical standards, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. The notification PDF is available on all official RRB websites.

  • Download RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), Ministry of Railways, Government of India
Advertisement No. CEN No. 01/2026
Post Name Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)
Department Ministry of Railways, Government of India (Various Zonal Railways)
Total Vacancies 11127 (All RRBs combined)
Pay Scale Level-2 in 7th CPC Pay Matrix; Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-
Medical Standard A-1 (Stringent vision and physical fitness standards; Lasik surgery candidates NOT eligible)
Application Mode Online only (via official RRB websites)
Selection Mode CBT-1 (75 marks) + CBT-2 (Part A: 100 marks + Part B: 75 marks qualifying) + CBAT + Document Verification + Medical Examination
Suitability for PwBD Not Suitable
Official Website Refer RRB-wise websites listed below (e.g., www.rrbald.gov.in, www.rrbmumbai.gov.in, etc.)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Indicative Notice in Employment News 21/03/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 15-05-2026 (00:00 Hrs.)
Last Date for Online Application Submission 14-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Last Date for Application Fee Payment 16-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Modification Window for Application Corrections 17-06-2026 to 26-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Age Calculated As On 01-07-2026
CBT-1 / CBT-2 / CBAT Date To be notified on official RRB websites

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 11127 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot across all Railway Recruitment Boards and Zonal Railways. These are distributed across UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS categories. Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) vacancies are included within the total vacancy count and are not separately additional. The post is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD).

Category No. of Posts
UR (Unreserved) 4860
SC (Scheduled Caste) 1746
ST (Scheduled Tribe) 919
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 2598
EWS (Economically Weaker Section) 1004
Total 11127
Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) - included in above total 1115

RRB & Zone-wise Vacancy Details (Selected RRBs)

RRB Zone UR SC ST OBC EWS Total ExSM
Ahmedabad WR 62 22 11 38 14 147 15
Ajmer NWR + WCR 318 115 59 174 52 718 72
Mumbai CR + SCR + WR 336 219 106 380 152 1193 120
Bhubaneswar ECoR 548 70 36 48 99 801 80
Guwahati NFR 272 109 63 164 68 676 68
Secunderabad ECoR + SCR 625 209 102 390 94 1420 143
Ranchi ECR + SER 472 233 105 363 127 1300 130
All RRBs Total 4860 1746 919 2598 1004 11127 1115

Note: Complete RRB & Zone-wise vacancy details are available in Annexure B of the official notification.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (14-06-2026). Educational/Technical qualification certificates must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute. Candidates awaiting results of their final examination for the prescribed qualification are NOT eligible to apply. The post of ALP is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD). Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for this post.

Educational Qualification

Option Details
Option A - Matriculation + ITI Matriculation / SSLC plus ITI from recognised institutions of NCVT/SCVT in the trades of Fitter, Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV), Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Wireman, Tractor Mechanic, Armature & Coil Winder, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning Mechanic. OR Matriculation / SSLC plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship in the above trades.
Option B - Matriculation + Diploma/Degree in Engineering Matriculation / SSLC plus 3-year Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Engineering (or combination of these streams) from a recognised institution in lieu of ITI. Note: Degree in the above Engineering disciplines will also be acceptable in lieu of Diploma.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age as on 01-07-2026. Age relaxation is available for various categories as detailed below. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation on more than one ground, only the maximum relaxation will be granted (not cumulative). SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR vacancies will not get any age relaxation. There is no fixed limit on the number of attempts - candidates can appear as long as they are within the prescribed upper age limit.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) & EWS 18 to 30 years (as on 01-07-2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
SC & ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
Ex-Servicemen (UR & EWS) 3 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (OBC-NCL) 6 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (SC & ST) 8 years after deduction of length of service from age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - UR & EWS Maximum 40 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - OBC (NCL) Maximum 43 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - SC & ST Maximum 45 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (UR & EWS) Maximum 35 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (OBC-NCL) Maximum 38 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (SC & ST) Maximum 40 years of age

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the non-refundable recruitment fee online. Fee can be paid only through online mode - internet banking, debit/credit cards, or UPI. Applications received without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Candidates who appear in CBT-1 will receive a refund of their examination fee (after deducting applicable bank charges) credited to their Aadhaar-seeded bank account. Candidates whose applications are rejected, or who do not appear in CBT-1, will not receive any refund.

Category Examination Fee Refund on Appearing in CBT-1
All Candidates (except categories below) Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred only) Rs. 400/- refunded (deducting bank charges)
SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen / Female / Transgender / Minorities / Economically Backward Class (EBC) Rs. 250/- (Rupees Two Hundred and Fifty only) Rs. 250/- fully refunded (deducting bank charges)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 consists of five successive stages - First Stage CBT (CBT-1), Second Stage CBT (CBT-2), Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT), Document Verification (DV), and Medical Examination (ME). CBT-1 is a screening exam only and its marks are not counted in the final panel. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of combined marks from Part-A of CBT-2 (50% weightage) and CBAT scores (50% weightage). Negative marking of 1/3rd mark per wrong answer applies in both CBT-1 and CBT-2. There is no negative marking in CBAT.

Stage 1 - First Stage CBT (CBT-1) - Screening Only

Details Particulars
Duration 60 Minutes
Total Questions / Marks 75 Questions / 75 Marks (1 mark per question)
Type Objective MCQ - Computer Based Test
Negative Marking 1/3rd mark deducted per wrong answer
Minimum Qualifying Marks UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Shortlisting for CBT-2 15 times the vacancies notified per RRB (based on CBT-1 merit, RRB-wise)
Syllabus Mathematics, Mental Ability (Reasoning), General Science (10th standard level)

Stage 2 - Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)

Part Duration Questions Nature Minimum Qualifying Marks
Part-A 90 Minutes 100 Questions / 100 Marks Competitive - marks counted for merit UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Part-B 60 Minutes 75 Questions / 75 Marks Qualifying only - trade-specific 35% for all candidates irrespective of category
Total Duration 175 Questions / 2.5 Hours  

Part-A Syllabus (CBT-2): Mathematics, General Intelligence and Reasoning, Basic Science and Engineering (Engineering Drawing, Units, Measurements, Basic Electricity, Levers, IT Literacy, etc.)

Part-B Syllabus (CBT-2) - Trade-wise Qualifying Test:

Sl. No. Qualification of Candidate Trade / Subject of Choice for Part-B
1 Electrical Engineering (and combinations) Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Wireman, Armature & Coil Winder, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
2 Electronics Engineering (and combinations) Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV)
3 Mechanical Engineering (and combinations) Fitter, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic, Heat Engine, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic
4 Automobile Engineering (and combinations) Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic

Stage 3 - Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)

Shortlisting for CBAT 8 times the ALP vacancies per community/category per RRB (based on Part-A marks of CBT-2)
Qualifying Marks Minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately (all candidates irrespective of category)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking

Stage 4 & 5 - Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (ME)

Final merit list is prepared RRB-wise from candidates qualifying in CBAT, with 50% weightage to Part-A of CBT-2 marks and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are shortlisted for DV and Medical Examination. In case of a tie, the older candidate is preferred; if age is also equal, alphabetical order of given name (A to Z) is applied.

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of given name (not surname)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Medical Standards

The post of ALP carries Medical Standard A-1. Candidates must be physically fit in all respects with stringent vision requirements. Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible. A Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Part-A signed by General Physician and Part-B signed by Eye Specialist, both from a Central/State Government Hospital or Medical College) must be submitted on the CBAT exam day.

Medical Standard General Fitness Vision Standards
A-1 Physically fit in all respects (i) Distant Vision: 6/6, 6/6 without glasses with fogging test (must not accept +2D); (ii) Near Vision: Sn: 0.6, 0.6 without glasses; (iii) Must pass tests for Colour Vision, Binocular Vision, Field of Vision, Night Vision, Mesopic Vision, etc.

How to Apply for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official websites of participating RRBs (listed at Para 13.7 of the CEN). Hard copy / offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal is active from 15-05-2026 to 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs.). Candidates must first create an account on the RRB portal. Those who created an account for CENs notified in 2024/2025 should use the same credentials. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Candidates can apply to only one RRB and submit only one application - multiple applications to the same or different RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and debarment from future RRB/RRC exams. Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication is strongly advised during account creation for a smoother recruitment process. A live photograph is captured during the online application; pre-existing photographs are not accepted. No documents need to be uploaded at application stage (except signature and SC/ST certificate for free travel pass, if applicable).

Documents to be Produced at Document Verification (on being called):

  • Age proof - Matriculation / SSLC / 10th class certificate
  • Educational qualifications (Matriculation onwards) - marksheets and certificates
  • ITI / Diploma / Degree certificate in prescribed trade / discipline
  • SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / Ex-Servicemen certificate (if applicable), in prescribed format and issued by competent authority
  • Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A & VIII B) - to be submitted on CBAT day
  • NOC from present employer (if applicable, for Government servants)

Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official RRB website for their chosen RRB for updates on all stages. Helpline for technical queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 0172-565-3333 and 9592001188 (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

Apply Online at: Visit the official website of your chosen RRB (see list below)

Important Instructions for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate is accepted - multiple applications to one or more RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and permanent debarment from RRB/RRC exams.
  • Candidates awaiting results of their final prescribed qualification examination are NOT eligible to apply.
  • Candidates who have undergone Lasik or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP.
  • Selection of RRB, once submitted, is final and cannot be changed at any later date.
  • Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation.
  • Examination fee is refundable (after deducting bank charges) only to candidates who appear in CBT-1; rejected or absent candidates are not eligible for refund.
  • Refund will be credited only to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account; candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account.
  • Mobile phones, electronic gadgets, watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, calculators, books, pen, food items, and all other listed banned items are strictly prohibited inside the test centre.
  • Examination centre, date, and shift allotted in the e-call letter are final - no requests for change will be entertained under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must maintain identical signatures throughout all stages of the recruitment process. No BLOCK/CAPITAL/DISJOINTED letter signatures are acceptable.
  • Free Sleeper Class Railway Pass is available for SC/ST candidates for CBT/CBAT/DV/Medical Examination stages (if opted in application).
  • Candidates are advised to visit only official RRB websites and beware of fake websites, unscrupulous agents, and touts claiming to provide Railway jobs through influence or unfair means. RRBs never appoint agents or coaching centres for recruitment.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest State Bank of India (SBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 19-05-2026 at the official website of SBI, this new recruitment drive offers 7150 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 19-05-2026 to 08-06-2026. Find all details about the SBI Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 7150 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official SBI website.

  • Download SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

SBI is recruiting 7150 Apprentice posts with Any Graduate qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), State Bank of India
Total Training Seats 7150 (across 34 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)

Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + SBI website)
Selection Mode Online Written Test + Test of Local Language + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Online Examination Tentatively in July 2026
Official Website https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

Local Language Test Date To be notified after Online Written Examination result

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 7150 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only.

Reservation for PwBD is horizontal within overall vacancies. Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description
Visual Impairment (VI) Blindness (total absence of sight / visual acuity less than 3/60 in better eye / field less than 10°) and Low Vision (visual acuity not exceeding 6/18 / field less than 40°)
Hearing Impaired (HI) Hard of Hearing – 60 DB to 70 DB hearing loss in speech frequencies in both ears
Locomotor Disability (LD) OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Muscular Dystrophy, Acid Attack Victims, Spinal Deformity (SD) and Spinal Injury (SI) without associated neurological/limb dysfunction
d & e Categories Autism Spectrum Disorder (Mild); Multiple Disabilities among LD, VI, HI, SLD, MI & ASD

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-04-2026). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online through SBI website (https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers).
Educational Qualification (as on 01-04-2026) Graduation from a recognized University / Institute.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either in SBI or in any other organization earlier, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after attainment of educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
CIBIL Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid outstanding till date of issuance of offer of engagement shall not be eligible.
Aadhaar Requirement All candidates (except those belonging to Northeastern States where alternate ID proof will do) should produce Aadhaar Number before engagement, i.e., contract signing for apprenticeship training.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-04-2026, i.e., candidates must have been born not later than 01-04-2006 and not earlier than 02-04-1998 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
PwBD As per Government of India guidelines

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for payment of fees. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
General / OBC / EWS Rs. 300/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD NIL

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on (i) Online Written Test and (ii) Test of Local Language, followed by Medical Examination. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, qualifying in local language test, and medical fitness. A Wait List (State-category wise) will be maintained for one year from the date of result or next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. Candidates will be released only once from this waitlist against non-joining subject to availability of qualified candidates.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Objective Type)

Sl. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
2 General English 25 25 15 minutes English only
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
4 Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
Total 100 100 60 minutes  

Each test has a separate timing as mentioned above. There will be NEGATIVE MARKS for wrong answers — 1/4th of the mark assigned for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer. Test questions (except General English) will be set in 13 Regional Languages viz. Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu — in addition to English and Hindi — as applicable State-wise. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order; tie-break is by age in descending order.

Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

The test of specified local language will be conducted after qualifying the online written examination. Candidates who fail to qualify this test will not be engaged as apprentice. Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified local language will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates must appear at their own expense; SBI will not reimburse travelling expenses.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank and satisfactory completion of KYE formalities.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Weightage in Junior Associate Recruitment

Weightage/Relaxation to apprentices in recruitment of Junior Associates shall be given as per the recruitment policy of the Bank as amended from time to time, to apprentices who successfully complete the apprenticeship training in SBI, subject to candidate's satisfactory conduct and performance during the entire training period. Successful completion refers to passing of the skill evaluation test (Trade test) and obtention of the joint National Apprenticeship Certificate issued by SBI-NSDC / BFSI-SSC.

Competition Level Analysis for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high due to 7150 seats being offered at the all-India level and the exam being open to any graduate, including those already preparing for bank and SSC exams. While the written test is of easy to moderate difficulty and slightly below SBI Clerk level, the large number of serious aspirants in high-population states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will push state-wise and category-wise cut-offs upward.

In practical terms, each seat is likely to attract dozens to over a hundred candidates in major states, making speed, accuracy, and sectional balance crucial to clearing the single-stage written exam followed by the local language test. However, the effective competition reduces at later stages because some candidates will fail to qualify the language test or be filtered out at the document verification, CIBIL, Aadhaar, and medical fitness stages, and SBI will finally select candidates strictly on state-wise and category-wise merit from those who clear all stages.

Who should NOT apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job in SBI; this is only a 1‑year apprenticeship with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment.
  • Candidates who already completed any Apprenticeship (in SBI or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates outside the age band 20–28 years as on the cut-off date (even 1 day younger/older is not eligible, except notified relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
  • Aspirants who do not have a Graduation degree from a recognised University/Institute by the specified date.
  • Candidates not fluent in the notified local language of the State/UT (reading, writing, speaking, understanding), and who are unlikely to clear the language test or do not have 10th/12th proof.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register on the NAPS portal before applying, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for SBI Apprentice.
  • Candidates with serious CIBIL/loan/credit‑card defaults who have not regularised or repaid outstanding dues by the time of offer; such profiles are normally rejected in bank recruitments.
  • Ex‑Servicemen / Disabled Ex‑Servicemen, where the notification clearly states no engagement / no reservation for them under Apprentice.
  • PwBD candidates whose disability type falls under “likely to improve” (temporary disability) where reservation benefits may not apply as per notification conditions.
  • Candidates who cannot produce valid Aadhaar (except where exempted, e.g., certain North‑Eastern states with alternate ID allowed).

Common mistakes / What to check before applying SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying in a wrong State or language where you cannot comfortably read, write and speak the specified local language.
  • Entering wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed later and mismatch with certificates at DV can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer applying as OBC instead of UR; only non‑creamy OBCs with valid, recent certificate get reservation.
  • Uploading blurred/incorrect photo, signature, thumb impression or declaration (wrong size, wrong format, someone else’s handwriting) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using invalid or inactive mobile number/email ID, causing failure to receive OTPs, registration details, call letters or further communication.
  • Submitting multiple applications for different states or with different details; SBI usually accepts only the last complete application and may forfeit extra fees.
  • Ignoring negative marking and over‑attempting in the exam, which can sharply reduce your score despite high attempts.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later protesting about absorption or permanent posting after apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online; no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and read the detailed guidelines on https://bfsissc.com before applying online through the SBI website.

Candidates should have a valid email ID and mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (as per Annexure-I):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink (240×240 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) — in candidate's own handwriting
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at the time of online application)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Examination / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) along with photo identity proof (Passport / Aadhaar / PAN Card / Driving License / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Identity Card issued by School or College / Gazetted Officer in official letterhead) in original and self-attested photocopy
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST candidates) — to be submitted at the time of engagement if called for
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed Government of India format, issued during the period 01-04-2026 to the date of joining (for OBC candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in the format prescribed by Government of India, valid for the year 2026-27 (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by a Competent Authority as per Government of India guidelines (for PwBD candidates)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (Graduation onwards)

Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to SBI. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter for Online Written Examination will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own cost; SBI will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.

Apply Online at: https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

For queries: Call 022-22820427 (11:00 AM to 06:00 PM on working days) or visit https://cgrs.ibps.in — mention "Engagement of Apprentice in SBI" in the subject.

Important Instructions for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. NOR IS THE SAME CONTRACT EMPLOYMENT. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of SBI and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for employees of the Bank.
  • SBI shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to Apprentices during and/or after the completion of the Apprenticeship period. After completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.
  • Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online on SBI website is required.
  • No Ex-Servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an Apprentice and there is no reservation for Ex-Servicemen in the Apprentice Engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General or General (LD/VI/HI/d&e) as applicable.
  • Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.
  • Candidates with CIBIL default record (unpaid loans/credit card dues) are not eligible for engagement until dues are regularized/repaid.
  • All candidates (except Northeastern State candidates) must produce Aadhaar Number before engagement/contract signing.
  • Not more than one application should be submitted; in case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • PwBD candidates with disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability) category are not eligible for reservation.
  • PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility: candidates with 40% or more disability having physical limitation to write are eligible. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at his/her own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination is allowed (whether availing scribe or not) for eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their registered email ID/mobile active for receiving call letters/advices etc.
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only — candidates should visit regularly.
  • No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. No Travel Expenses will be reimbursed/paid by SBI.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.
  • Bank reserves right to cancel the selection process partly/entirely at any stage.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways have released an indicative Centralised Employment Notification (CEN No. 02/2026) for the recruitment of Technician Grade I Signal and various Technician Grade III posts. Applications will be accepted through online mode only, and a total of 6,565 Technician vacancies are expected to be filled across all RRBs for the 2026-27 recruitment cycle.

FOLLOW US

RRB Technician Notification 2026 Overview

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs)
Post Name Technician Grade I Signal & Technician Grade III
No of Posts 6,565 (Tentative)
Job Type Railway Jobs / Central Government Jobs
Pay Level (7th CPC) Technician Grade I Signal – Level 5, Technician Grade III – Level 2
Initial Pay Technician Grade I Signal – Rs. 29,200/-, Technician Grade III – Rs. 19,900/-
Medical Standard As per CEN (B-1 and other standards as notified)
Age Limit 18–36 Years (Tech. Grade I Signal), 18–33 Years (Tech. Grade III)
Opening Date of Application 30-06-2026
Closing Date for Submission of Application 29-07-2026 (23:59 hrs)
Short Notice RRB Technician Indicative Notice CEN 02/2026
Official Website https://www.rrbcdg.gov.in (and all regional RRB websites)

Important Dates

  • Indicative Notice Date: 23-05-2026
  • Opening Date of Online Application: 30-06-2026
  • Closing Date for Submission of Application: 29-07-2026 (23:59 hrs)
  • CBT Exam Schedule: 06, 09, 10 & 13 March 2026 (as per revised CBT schedule for CEN 02/2025)

RRB Technician Vacancy 2026 – Post Details

As per the approved intent, 6,565 Technician vacancies are proposed for the RRB Technician 2026-27 recruitment, distributed as follows across all RRBs. 

  • Technician Grade I Signal – 323 Posts
  • Technician Grade III – 6,242 Posts
  • Total Vacancies (All RRBs) – 6,565 Posts

Who Can Apply for RRB Technician 2026?

Eligibility criteria depend on the post and level. Candidates must meet educational, technical, and age requirements as per the Centralised Employment Notification.

  • Technician Grade I Signal: B.Sc. (Physics / Electronics / Computer Science / IT / Instrumentation) or Degree/Diploma in Engineering in relevant discipline from a recognized institution.
  • Technician Grade III: 10th / Matriculation plus ITI from NCVT/SCVT in relevant trade or Act Apprenticeship in the concerned trade.
  • Other Conditions: Candidates must satisfy prescribed medical standards and citizenship norms and should be within the specified age limit as on the date mentioned in CEN 02/2026.

Age Limit

  • Technician Grade I Signal: 18 to 36 Years
  • Technician Grade III: 18 to 33 Years
  • Upper age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/NCL/EWS/Ex-Servicemen/ PWBD) will be applicable as per Government of India rules and details will be given in the detailed CEN. 

Selection Process

RRB Technician recruitment involves multiple stages including a Computer Based Test and subsequent document and medical verification. There is no Computer Based Aptitude Test for these Technician posts.

  • Stage 1: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Stage 2: Document Verification
  • Stage 3: Medical Examination

RRB Technician Exam Pattern 2026

  • Mode of Exam: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Questions: 100 Objective Type Questions
  • Total Marks: 100 Marks
  • Exam Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: 1/3rd mark will be deducted for each wrong answer; no negative marks for unattempted questions. 

Salary Details

  • Technician Grade I Signal: Pay Level 5 (7th CPC) – Initial Pay Rs. 29,200/-
  • Technician Grade III: Pay Level 2 (7th CPC) – Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-
  • Additional allowances such as DA, HRA, TA and other benefits will be admissible as per Railway rules for the respective pay levels. 

How to Apply for RRB Technician Recruitment 2026

  • Visit your regional RRB website (e.g., Ahmedabad, Ajmer, Bengaluru, Chennai, Secunderabad, etc.).
  • Open the link for Centralised Employment Notification CEN 02/2026 – Technician.
  • Read the detailed notification carefully including post-wise eligibility, instructions and list of RRBs.
  • Register by providing basic details like name, mobile number and email ID.
  • Login using the registration credentials and fill the online application form for the desired post and pay level (only one RRB and one pay level allowed per candidate).
  • Upload required documents, photograph and signature as per specifications. >Pay the prescribed application fee through online mode, if applicable.
  • Submit the application form and take a printout / save PDF for future reference.

Important Points Mentioned in Indicative Notice

  • Each candidate can apply to only one RRB for a specific pay level and only one common online application is allowed for all notified posts in that pay level.
  • Separate applications must be submitted for each pay level, if eligible.
  • Candidates are strongly advised to verify name, date of birth and other details using Aadhaar to avoid mismatches at later stages.
  • RRBs caution candidates to be aware of touts, brokers and job racketeers and to rely only on official RRB websites for genuine information.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Punjab & Sind Bank (PSB) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 21-05-2026 at the official website of Punjab & Sind Bank, this new recruitment drive offers 635 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 21-05-2026 to 06-06-2026. Find all details about the Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, NBCC Building Block 3, Kidwai Nagar, New Delhi – 110023 has officially released a notification dated 21/05/2026 for engagement of 635 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Punjab & Sind Bank website.

  • Download Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Punjab & Sind Bank is recruiting 635 Apprentice posts with Regular Graduation qualification, last date 06 June 2026.

Organization Name Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, New Delhi

Total Training Seats 635 (across 29 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 12,300/- per month (no daily allowance / conveyance / travelling allowance)
Duration of Training 12 Months (On-the-Job Training; 10:00 AM to 05:00 PM)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + NATS portal + PSB website)
Selection Mode Merit-based on HSC / 10+2 marks (State-wise, District-wise, Category-wise) + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration with 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/)
Official Website https://punjabandsind.bank.in/

Age Calculated As On 01-05-2026
Educational Qualification Cut-off Date 06-06-2026

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 635 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the respective cut-off dates. Only candidates with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals are eligible to apply through the Bank's website. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District and must not submit more than one application.

Criteria Details
Nationality / Citizenship Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal or Bhutan / Tibetan Refugee (came before 01-01-1962) / Person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable.
Portal Registration (Mandatory) 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/) before applying on PSB website.
Educational Qualification (as on 06-06-2026) Regular Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government.
Post Graduate Candidates Candidates who have completed Post Graduate qualification are NOT eligible.
Work Experience Candidates with work experience of 1 year or more after educational qualification are NOT eligible.
Years Since Qualifying Exam Candidates should not have completed 5 years after passing the qualifying examination.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing Apprenticeship in any organisation under the Apprentices Act 1961 are NOT eligible.
Local Language Proficiency The candidate must be proficient in the local language (Reading, Writing, Speaking & Understanding) of the applied State/UT and must produce 8th/10th/12th or Graduate level certificate evidencing having studied the local language.
Aadhaar Requirement Every apprentice must have a valid Aadhaar number before engagement / contract signing.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

SC / ST 5 years relaxation
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
Persons With Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) 10 years relaxation
Widows / Divorced Women / Women legally separated (not remarried) Up to 35 years (General/EWS), 38 years (OBC), 40 years (SC/ST)
Persons affected by 1984 riots 5 years relaxation

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
SC / ST / PwBD Rs. 100/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 200/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be merit-based on HSC/10+2 marks. No written examination is conducted. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, district-wise vacancy availability, and medical fitness. In case of a tie in marks, candidates will be ranked by age in descending order within their respective category, state, and district.

Stage 1 - Merit List (Based on HSC / 10+2 Marks)

Candidates who fulfill minimum eligibility criteria will be placed in a merit list in descending order of their 10+2 marks, drawn State-wise, District-wise, and Category-wise. No written examination is conducted for this recruitment. Candidates must select their preferred State and District in the application itself. In case of equal marks, tie-break is by age in descending order within the respective category, state, and district.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through email only and must appear for document verification at their own expense. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Candidates failing to produce originals will not be allowed to proceed further.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - District-wise Vacancy Details

Candidates must apply for one specific district within their chosen State. District-wise allotment will be subject to vacancy availability and administrative requirements of the Bank.

Uttarakhand 10 Dehradun, Haridwar 5, 5 Hindi

Competition Level Analysis for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for PSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. With only 635 seats spread across 29 States/UTs, the vacancy count is significantly lower than comparable bank apprenticeship drives such as SBI. Selection is purely merit-based on 10+2 marks without any written examination, which means candidates with high HSC scores - especially from states like Uttar Pradesh (100 seats), Punjab (90 seats), Bihar (40 seats), Maharashtra (30 seats), and Haryana (50 seats) - will face intense competition within their district and category.

Since there is no written exam, the effective competition depends entirely on the candidate's 10+2 percentage and age as a tie-breaker. Attrition at later stages will occur through local language proof verification, document verification, and medical fitness, which will reduce the final eligible pool. Candidates who do not have proof of studying the local language at 8th/10th/12th level are at a disadvantage as they must demonstrate local language proficiency separately.

Who should NOT apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job; this is a 12-month apprenticeship only, with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already completed any Apprenticeship (in PSB or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates who have completed Post Graduate (PG) qualification - PG holders are explicitly not eligible.
  • Candidates with 1 year or more of work/job experience after completion of educational qualification.
  • Candidates who have completed more than 5 years after passing their qualifying examination.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 20–28 years as on 01-05-2026, except those eligible for notified relaxation under SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/widow/1984 riot victim categories.
  • Aspirants who do not hold a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute by 06-06-2026.
  • Candidates not proficient in the local language of the State/UT they are applying for, and who cannot produce 8th/10th/12th or graduation-level certificate evidencing study of that language.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register with 100% completed profile on both NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and NATS (nats.education.gov.in) portals before applying - this is a mandatory prerequisite.
  • Candidates without a valid Aadhaar number, as it is mandatory before engagement/contract signing.
  • Candidates applying with a Ration Card or Learner's Driving License as ID proof - these are not accepted as valid identity documents for this process.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Not completing 100% profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying; incomplete portal profiles will render the candidate ineligible.
  • Applying for a wrong State or District where the candidate is not proficient in the local language and cannot produce language study certificates.
  • Entering the wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed after submission and any mismatch at document verification can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from the creamy layer applying under OBC reservation instead of UR; only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates with a valid, recent certificate are entitled to reservation.
  • Uploading blurred or incorrect photograph, signature, thumb impression or handwritten declaration (wrong size, format, or written by someone else) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using an invalid or inactive mobile number or email ID, resulting in failure to receive OTPs, shortlisting intimations, or further communication from the Bank.
  • Submitting multiple applications; only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees paid for other registrations will be forfeited.
  • Signing the application in CAPITAL LETTERS; signatures in capital letters are not acceptable and must match across all documents and correspondence.
  • Forgetting to note down the Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal, which is mandatory to be mentioned while applying for training seats.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later disputing about permanent absorption after the 12-month apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the Bank's website (https://punjabandsind.bank.in/) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must mandatorily complete 100% registration on both the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/). Candidates must also note down their Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal as it is required while applying.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (to be signed across by the candidate on printing)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be accepted)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink - text: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at time of online application)
  • Image files must be in .jpg / .jpeg format; scanned documents must be in PDF format only

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:

  • Xth / Date of Birth certificate in support of Date of Birth
  • Printout of application form
  • All certificates and mark sheets of educational qualification from Standard Xth, XII, and Graduation onwards
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format for SC/ST candidates
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format, valid as on date of joining (issued within one year prior to joining); OBC Caste Name must tally with Central Government list
  • Income and Asset Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority, valid EWS certificate for the financial year, for EWS category candidates
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board for PwBD candidates
  • Age relaxation proof with valid documents (e.g., District Magistrate certificate for 1984 riot-affected persons)
  • Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Permanent Driving License / Voter’s Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Aadhaar / E-Aadhaar / Identity Card issued by recognised College/University / Gazetted Officer certificate) - bearing the same name as on the application / call letter. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are NOT valid.
  • Two passport-size photographs similar to the one used during portal registration
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

NO DOCUMENT SHOULD BE DIRECTLY SENT TO THE BANK BEFORE OR AFTER THE PROCESS. Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to the Bank. Candidates must appear for document verification at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ → Home Page → Recruitment → Relevant Notification

For queries: Email [email protected] | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only.

Important Instructions for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of the Bank and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for Bank employees.
  • The Bank shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to any Apprentice who has completed the period of training, nor shall the Apprentice be obligated to accept employment. The Apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training.
  • Candidates can apply for only one State and one District, and not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fees for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • Mandatory registration with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying on the Bank's website is required.
  • Candidates who had training or job experience of one or more years after completion of educational qualifications shall not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General/UR.
  • Change of address / email ID / phone number / caste / category will not be entertained after submission of the application.
  • Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be acceptable. The signature must be consistent across all documents and correspondence.
  • No daily allowance, conveyance allowance or travelling allowance will be paid to Apprentices. No overtime is permitted. Apprentices are entitled to only 1 casual leave per month (maximum 4 at a time).
  • The Contract of Apprenticeship shall terminate automatically on expiry of the 12-month training period. A non-performing apprentice will be given 7 days' notice. An apprentice wishing to terminate early must give 15 days' prior notice to the Bank.
  • Apprentices must devote their entire time exclusively to the Bank and shall not engage in any other commercial business, service, or advisory activity during the engagement period.
  • Apprentices must not disclose any financial information, data, concepts or internal communications relating to the Bank's business to any unauthorized person.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resultant dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at Delhi.
  • Bank reserves the right to change/add/cancel the selection process or cancel the entire process at any stage without notice or refund of application fees.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only - candidates should visit regularly.
