📄 Complete Details & Notification

National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO) Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Notification has been released on the official website nalcoindia.com. A total of 272 vacancies have been announced through the NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026.

FOLLOW US

Candidates meeting the prescribed age criteria and possessing qualifications such as 10th, ITI, Diploma, B.Sc. (Chemistry), Mining Mate Certificate, Mining Foreman Certificate, or relevant trade qualifications are eligible to apply for these Non-Executive vacancies. The online application process for NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 will start from 21-05-2026 and will remain open until 10-06-2026.

Candidates can download the official notification PDF and apply online through the direct links provided below in this article.

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO), a Navratna Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE) and one of the largest integrated Alumina-Aluminium companies in Asia, has officially released Advertisement No. 10260213 dated 14.05.2026 for recruitment of Non-Executive Personnel at Mines & Refinery (M&R) Complex, Damanjodi, Koraput District, Odisha. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, emoluments, PwBD sub-categories, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. All further updates and corrigenda will be published on the official NALCO website.

  • Download NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name National Aluminium Company Limited (NALCO) - A Navratna Government of India Enterprise
Advertisement No. 10260213 dated 14.05.2026
Post Names Operator (HEMM) Gr. III, Mining Mate Gr. III, Jr. Foreman (Mines), Jr. Foreman (Electrical), SUPT(JOT)-Operator, Operator Gr. III, SUPT(JOT)-Fitter, Technician (Fitter) Gr. III, SUPT(JOT)-Electrical, Technician (Electrical) Gr. III, SUPT(JOT)-Laboratory, SUPT(JOT)-Instrumentation/Instrument Mechanic, Technician (Instrumentation/Instrument Mechanic) Gr. III
Department / Location Mines & Refinery (M&R) Complex, Damanjodi, Koraput District, Odisha
Application Mode Online only (via www.nalcoindia.com)
Selection Mode Computer Based Test (CBT) + Trade Test (for select posts) + Document Verification + Pre-employment Medical Examination (PEME)
Pay Scale (T0 Grade) Rs. 29,500 – 3% – 70,000/- (Induction Level)
Pay Scale (S0 Grade) Rs. 36,500 – 3% – 1,15,000/- (Induction Level for Jr. Foreman posts)
Official Website https://www.nalcoindia.com

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Opening of Online Application Submission 21-05-2026 (10:00 Hrs.)
Last Date for Online Application Submission and Fee Payment (with upload of all relevant documents) 10-06-2026 (upto 23:55 Hrs.)
Age Calculated As On 10-06-2026 (last date of online application submission)
CBT / Trade Test Date To be notified on NALCO website and through email/SMS

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

Vacancies are distributed across UR, SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and EWS categories. In addition to category-wise reservation, 20 posts (including 10 backlog posts) are horizontally reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) as per Government guidelines, and 38 posts are horizontally reserved for Ex-Servicemen (ESM) as per Government guidelines.

Sl. No. Post Grade & Pay Scale Total Posts
1 Operator (HEMM) Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 6
2 Mining Mate Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 21
3 Jr. Foreman (Mines) S0 grade (Rs. 36500–3%–115000/-) 8
4 Jr. Foreman (Electrical) S0 grade (Rs. 36500–3%–115000/-) 3
5 SUPT(JOT)-Operator SUPT → JOT → T0 grade 55
6 Operator Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 54
7 SUPT(JOT)-Fitter SUPT → JOT → T0 grade 12
8 Technician (Fitter) Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 13
9 SUPT(JOT)-Electrical SUPT → JOT → T0 grade 35
10 Technician (Electrical) Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 35
11 SUPT(JOT)-Laboratory SUPT → JOT-Lab → T0 grade 6
12 SUPT(JOT)-Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic SUPT → JOT → T0 grade 10
13 Technician (Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic) Gr. III T0 grade (Rs. 29500–3%–70000/-) 10

Note: 20 posts (including 10 backlog) are horizontally reserved for PwBD, and 38 posts are horizontally reserved for Ex-Servicemen (ESM), as per Government guidelines. A candidate is allowed to apply for only one post.

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (10-06-2026). Qualifications must be from Universities or Institutes recognized/accredited by UGC/AICTE/NCVT/NCVET/SCTE&VT set up by Central/State Government. Only full-time qualifications will be considered. ITI/Diploma/B.Sc. in the specific branch/subjects as mentioned against respective posts will be considered as eligible qualifications; ITI/Diploma/B.Sc. in other branches will not be considered. No claim of possession of equivalent qualifications will be entertained.

Educational Qualification (Post-wise)

 

Operator (HEMM) Gr. III

  • 10th Pass with ITI (NCVT/NCVET) of 2 years duration in Mechanic Motor Vehicle (MMV) trade OR 1 year duration in Diesel Mechanic Trade
  • Apprenticeship Certificate in MMV or Diesel Mechanic Trade required
  • Valid Heavy Vehicle Driving Licence mandatory
  • First Aid Training Certificate desirable

Mining Mate Gr. III

  • 10th Pass
  • Valid Mining Mate Certificate of Competency (Restricted to Opencast Metalliferous Mines) issued by DGMS
  • First Aid Certificate preferable

Jr. Foreman (Mines)

  • Diploma in Mining / Mining Engineering
  • Valid Mining Foreman Certificate of Competency (Restricted to Opencast Metalliferous Mines) issued by DGMS

Jr. Foreman (Electrical)

  • Full-time Diploma in Electrical Engineering from a recognized University/Institution
  • Valid Supervisory Certificate of Competency (M.V/H.T/E.H.T) issued by the Electrical Licensing Board, Odisha covering Mining installations

SUPT (JOT)-Operator & Operator Gr. III

  • 10th Pass with ITI (NCVT/NCVET) in Electronics Mechanic / Technician Mechatronics / Electrician / Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic / Fitter Trade (2 years duration)
  • Apprenticeship Certificate required

SUPT (JOT)-Fitter & Technician (Fitter) Gr. III

  • 10th Pass with ITI (NCVT/NCVET) in Fitter Trade (2 years duration)
  • Apprenticeship Certificate required

SUPT (JOT)-Electrical & Technician (Electrical) Gr. III

  • 10th Pass with ITI (NCVT/NCVET) in Electrician Trade (2 years duration)
  • Apprenticeship Certificate required

SUPT (JOT)-Laboratory

  • B.Sc. (Hons) in Chemistry

SUPT (JOT)-Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic & Technician (Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic) Gr. III

  • 10th Pass with ITI (NCVT/NCVET) in Instrumentation / Instrument Mechanic Trade (2 years duration)
  • Apprenticeship Certificate required

** If the Supervisory Certificate of Competency (M.V/H.T/E.H.T) is granted by any other Electrical Licensing Board, the candidate must get it endorsed for validity from the Electrical Licensing Board of Odisha as per the Electrical Licensing Board Regulation, Odisha, 2014, failing which his service will not be confirmed.

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age is calculated as on the last date of online application submission (10-06-2026). Upper age limit is relaxable for various categories. The maximum upper age limit including all relaxations shall not exceed 56 years. The relaxation in age for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) is subject to availability of vacancy for the particular category.

Post Type General (UR) / EWS OBC (NCL) SC / ST PwBD - UR PwBD - OBC (NCL) PwBD - SC / ST
SUPT(JOT) posts (Fresher) 27 years 30 years 32 years 37 years 40 years 42 years
Operator / Technician / Mining Mate Gr. III posts (Experienced) 35 years 38 years 40 years 45 years 48 years 50 years
Jr. Foreman posts (Experienced) 40 years 43 years 45 years 50 years 53 years 55 years
Land Ousted Candidates (M&R Complex, Damanjodi) Maximum 40 years as on 10-06-2026 (all categories); age relaxation applicable as above additionally

Additional relaxations: Ex-Servicemen and children/family members of those who died in the 1984 riots will get relaxation as per Government of India directives.

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee is non-refundable under any circumstances. Fee can be paid through dedicated bank account, net banking, debit card, or UPI only. Fee shall not be collected by any other mode. Candidates are advised to verify their eligibility before paying the fee. SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Land Ousted candidates, and internal candidates are exempted from payment of application fee.

Category Application Fee
General (UR) / OBC (NCL) / EWS candidates Rs. 100/- (Rupees One Hundred only) - Non-refundable
SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen / Land Ousted / Internal Candidates Exempted (No fee)

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process consists of Computer Based Test (CBT) and/or Trade Test, followed by Document Verification and Pre-employment Medical Examination (PEME). For posts at Sl. No. 5, 7, 9, 11, and 12 of the vacancy table, selection will be based on CBT only. For posts at Sl. No. 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 10, and 13, selection will be made through both CBT and Trade Test, with a weightage of 60% for CBT and 40% for Trade Test. There is no negative marking for wrong answers. CBT will be conducted in English and Hindi only.

Computer Based Test (CBT) Details

Type Objective type - 100 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs), 1 mark each
Duration 120 minutes (2 hours)
Question Paper Composition 60% Technical (domain specific) questions + 40% Non-technical questions (Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, General Knowledge and English)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking

Selection Mode - Post-wise

Posts Selection Mode CBT Weightage Trade Test Weightage
SUPT(JOT)-Operator, SUPT(JOT)-Fitter, SUPT(JOT)-Electrical, SUPT(JOT)-Laboratory, SUPT(JOT)-Instrumentation/Instrument Mechanic (Sl. No. 5, 7, 9, 11, 12) CBT only 100% -
Operator (HEMM) Gr. III, Mining Mate Gr. III, Jr. Foreman (Mines), Jr. Foreman (Electrical), Operator Gr. III, Technician (Fitter) Gr. III, Technician (Electrical) Gr. III, Technician (Instrumentation) Gr. III (Sl. No. 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 10, 13) CBT + Trade Test 60% 40%

Tentative CBT Cities (Annexure-II)

Sl. No. State City / Cities
1 Andhra Pradesh Visakhapatnam
2 Assam Guwahati
3 Maharashtra Mumbai / Navi Mumbai / Thane / MMR region
4 Delhi Delhi / NCR
5 Odisha Angul, Balasore, Berhampur-Ganjam, Bhawanipatna, Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Jeypore, Koraput, Rourkela, Sambalpur
6 Tamil Nadu Chennai
7 West Bengal Kolkata

NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 - Emoluments & Benefits

Stipend during SUPT Training (12 months) Rs. 12,000/- per month
Stipend during JOT Training - First 12 months Rs. 15,000/- per month
Stipend during JOT Training - Remaining period Rs. 15,500/- per month
Medical Facility during Training Indoor and Outdoor at NALCO Hospital
On Placement (T0 Grade) Rs. 29,500 – 3% – 70,000/- with all regular employee emoluments
Other Benefits on Placement Transport Allowance, HRA / Housing, Site Location Allowance, Leave Travel Allowance, Medical Facility (CHSS), Educational facilities, PF, Gratuity, Group Insurance, Pension (NALCO Defined Contribution Pension Scheme 2025), Performance Linked Incentive Scheme, etc.

How to Apply for NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official NALCO website https://www.nalcoindia.com. Applications received through any other mode will not be accepted and will be summarily rejected. The online application is a 2-step process -

Step-I: Registration and profile creation

Step-II: Login with credentials, fill application form, upload documents, and make online payment of fee (if applicable). Candidates must carefully furnish all correct details as no change/edit will be allowed after submission. A candidate is allowed to apply for only one post; if multiple applications are submitted for a single post, the latest application will be considered and the rest rejected. If a candidate applies for multiple posts, the candidature will be summarily rejected. Before applying online, candidates must scan and keep ready: passport-size photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm, 20–50 KB), signature (blue/black ink, 10–20 KB), left thumb impression, hand-written declaration, and all relevant certificates.

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Left Thumb Impression
  • Hand Written Declaration (in English, blue/black ink)
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Matriculation Certificate / School Leaving Certificate / Matriculation mark sheet) (PDF)
  • Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Driving Licence / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Photo ID issued by Gazetted Officer) (PDF)
  • Educational Certificates - All mark sheets and certificates (Final or Provisional) of Class 10th, ITI, Graduation, Diploma (as applicable) (PDF)
  • Ex-Servicemen eligibility proof with declarations as per latest Government guidelines (if applicable) (PDF)
  • Valid Employment Exchange Registration Card
  • Valid Heavy Vehicle Driving Licence (for Sl. No. 1 - Operator HEMM post)
  • Valid Mining Foreman Certificate of Competency / Electrical Supervisor's Certificate of Competency / Mining Mate Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS (as applicable for the post applied for)
  • National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) with requisite endorsement
  • Latest SC / ST / Disability Certificate / OBC (NCL) Certificate / EWS - Income & Asset Certificate, issued on or after 01-04-2026, in prescribed format (PDF)
  • Experience Certificate (if applicable) in the format given at Annexure-IV, on letterhead of the Organisation, along with offer of appointment and last pay slip
  • Land Ousted candidates - all supporting documents including land documents, legal heir certificate, and affidavit in prescribed format (Annexure-III) on non-judicial stamp paper of Rs. 25/-

Apply Online at: https://www.nalcoindia.com → Career page → Current Openings → Recruitment of Non-Executive Personnel – M&R Complex, Damanjodi-2026 → View Advertisement → Click here to Apply Online

For any queries: https://ccrs.ibps.in or Toll-free Helpline No. 1800222366 or 18001034566 (09:30 AM to 6:00 PM on working days except Saturday, Sunday, and holidays).

Important Instructions for NALCO Non-Executive Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • A candidate is allowed to apply for only one post - applications for multiple posts will result in summary rejection of all applications.
  • Only full-time qualifications from recognized institutes will be considered; ITI/Diploma/B.Sc. must be in the specific branch/trade prescribed for the post.
  • All qualifications and experience must have been acquired on or before the closing date of application (10-06-2026).
  • OBC (NCL) certificate must be issued on or after 01-04-2026 (Central List OBC only - State Government OBC lists are not followed).
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate must be issued on or after 01-04-2026 based on FY 2025-26 income.
  • PwBD candidates must possess a Disability Certificate certifying minimum 40% disability issued as per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules, 2017. Only permanent (non-temporary) disabilities are eligible for reservation and age relaxation.
  • Land Ousted candidates whose lands have been acquired for the NALCO project at Damanjodi must upload a valid Employment Preference Certificate - maximum age is 40 years as on 10-06-2026.
  • Employees of PSUs/Government organizations must apply through proper channel and produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) at the time of document verification.
  • Candidates working in PSUs/Government organizations who fail to produce NOC will not be considered for appointment; their application will be subject to submission of original release order.
  • Application fee once paid shall neither be refunded under any circumstances nor held in reserve for any other recruitment or selection.
  • No documents/certificates and application forms are required to be sent by post.
  • Signature / hand written declaration in CAPITAL LETTERS will NOT be accepted.
  • Electronic gadgets including mobile phones, smartwatches, etc. are not permitted inside the CBT examination hall - carrying them may lead to criminal prosecution.
  • Canvassing in any form during any stage of recruitment will render the candidate ineligible.
  • Any dispute with regard to this recruitment will be settled within the jurisdiction of Cuttack (Odisha) only.
  • NALCO does not seek payment of any kind other than the above-mentioned application fee - candidates are cautioned against fraud.

The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released the official National Defence Academy and Naval Academy (NDA & NA) Examination II Notification 2026 on 20-05-2026 at upsc.gov.in. A total of 394 vacancies have been announced under the UPSC NDA II 2026 Recruitment. Eligible candidates aged between 01-01-2008 and 01-01-2011 with a qualification of 12th Class pass (with Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics for Navy, Air Force, and INAC) can apply online from 20-05-2026 to 09-06-2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

FOLLOW US

UPSC NDA 2 2026 Notification PDF

Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has officially released Examination Notice No. 10/2026-NDA-II dated 20/05/2026 for the National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (II), 2026 to be held on 13.09.2026. The notification includes complete details on service-wise vacancies, eligibility conditions, scheme of examination, syllabus, selection process, exam centres, application procedure, and terms and conditions of service. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official UPSC website.

  • Download UPSC NDA 2 2026 Official Notification PDF

UPSC NDA 2 2026 Overview

UPSC is conducting NDA & NA Examination II, 2026 for 394 vacancies across Army, Navy, and Air Force wings of NDA and the Naval Academy, with last date 09 June 2026.

Organization Name Union Public Service Commission (UPSC), New Delhi
Examination Name National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (II), 2026
Examination Notice No. 10/2026-NDA-II dated 20/05/2026
Course / Academy NDA 158th Course (Army, Navy, Air Force) commencing 01-07-2027; 120th INAC (10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme) commencing 01-07-2027
Total Vacancies 394 (370 Male + 24 Female; provisional)
Stipend During Training Rs. 56,100/- per month (starting pay in Level 10)
Application Mode Online only via https://upsconline.nic.in
Selection Mode Written Examination (Objective Type) + SSB Intelligence & Personality Test (including CPSS for Air Force Flying) + Medical Examination
Date of Examination 13.09.2026
Written Result (Tentative) October 2026
SSB Interview (Tentative) December 2026 – January 2027
Course Commencement 01-07-2027
Official Website https://upsc.gov.in / https://upsconline.nic.in

UPSC NDA 2 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Notification 20/05/2026
Opening of Online Application 20-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application 09-06-2026 (up to 6:00 PM)
Helpline Active Period 20-05-2026 to 09-06-2026 (10:00 AM to 5:30 PM, working days)
e-Admit Card Download Last working day of the preceding week of the examination date
Date of Written Examination 13-09-2026
Representation Window for Answer Keys 3rd day to 7th day (up to 6:00 PM) after examination
Written Result (Tentative) October 2026
SSB Interview (Tentative) December 2026 – January 2027
Submission of 10+2 Certificate (for appearing candidates) By 10-06-2027
Course Commencement (NDA & INAC) 01-07-2027

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 394 vacancies have been announced across the Army, Navy, and Air Force wings of NDA (158th Course) and the Indian Naval Academy (120th INAC, 10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme). Vacancies are provisional and may change depending on the availability of training capacity. Written results and final merit lists for men and women are prepared separately as per vacancies notified.

Academy Service / Branch Male Vacancies Female Vacancies Total
National Defence Academy (NDA) – 158th Course Army 198 10 208
Navy (All Executive Branch) 37 05 42
Air Force – Flying 90 02 92
Air Force – Ground Duties (Tech) 16 02 18
Air Force – Ground Duties (Non-Tech) 08 02 10
Naval Academy (INAC) – 120th Course 10+2 Cadet Entry Scheme (All Executive Branch) 21 03 24
Total 370 24 394

Note: Candidates must specify clearly their service preferences (1 to 4) in the Examination Specific Module. They will be considered only for the services for which they exercise preference. No addition/alteration in preferences is entertained after submission. At least one preference is mandatory to be considered for service allocation.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions before the date of application. Their admission at all stages is purely provisional subject to satisfying eligibility conditions. Verification of eligibility with reference to original documents will be taken up only after the candidate has qualified for SSB/Personality Test.

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal / Person of Indian origin migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia) or Vietnam - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable. Gorkha subjects of Nepal do not need such certificate.
Marital Status Unmarried male and female candidates only. Candidates must undertake not to marry until they complete their full training. A candidate who marries after applying will not be selected, and a candidate who marries during training will be discharged and must refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.
Educational Qualification – Army Wing (NDA) 12th Class pass (or appearing) of the 10+2 pattern from a State Education Board or University or equivalent.
Educational Qualification – Navy Wing, Air Force Wing (NDA) and INAC (Naval Academy) 12th Class pass (or appearing) with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics of the 10+2 pattern from a State Education Board or University or equivalent.
Appearing Candidates Candidates appearing in 12th Class can also apply but must submit proof of passing (Matriculation and 10+2 certificate) to Army HQ / Naval HQ by 10-06-2027. Candidates studying in 11th Class are NOT eligible.
CPSS / PABT (Air Force Flying) Candidates who have previously failed CPSS/PABT are not eligible for the Flying Branch of Air Force. A candidate who fails CPSS can be considered for Air Force Ground Duty branches if willing. CPSS is conducted only once; grade secured holds good for every subsequent Air Force Selection Board interview.
Disqualification Candidates who have resigned or been withdrawn from any training academy of Armed Forces on disciplinary grounds or for lack of officer-like qualities are not eligible. Candidates debarred by the Ministry of Defence from holding any commission are also not eligible.
Physical Standards Candidates must be physically and mentally fit as per the "Joint Order Manual of Medical Standards for Entry into Armed Forces (1st Edition)" available at www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. Permanent body tattoos allowed only on inner forearm (inside elbow to wrist) and back/dorsal of palm; not permitted on any other body part.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 Application Form Date

The online application form for UPSC NDA 2 2026 will be available from 20 May 2026 to 09 June 2026 on the official UPSC online portal. Candidates need to complete registration, fill the form, upload documents, and submit the fee (if applicable) within this window, as applications will not be accepted after 6:00 PM on the last date.

  • Application start date: 20 May 2026
  • Last date to apply: 09 June 2026 (up to 6:00 PM)
  • Mode of application: Online only
  • Official website: https://upsconline.nic.in

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Age Limit & Marital Status

Only unmarried male and female candidates born not earlier than 01-01-2008 and not later than 01-01-2011 are eligible. Age is accepted only as recorded in the Matriculation / Secondary School Leaving Certificate. No other document (horoscope, affidavit, birth extract from Municipal Corporation, service record, etc.) will be accepted. Once a date of birth is submitted, no change is allowed subsequently under any circumstance.

Category Age Limit Date of Birth Range
All Candidates (Male & Female, Unmarried) Born not earlier than 01-01-2008 and not later than 01-01-2011 02-01-2008 to 01-01-2011 (both days inclusive)

Note: There is no upper age relaxation for any category (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) in NDA & NA Examination. The age band is uniformly applicable to all candidates.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Application Fee

Fee is payable through online mode only (Net Banking / Visa / Master / RuPay Credit / Debit Card / UPI). Payment through any other mode is not valid. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination.

Category Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male) Rs. 100/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST Candidates NIL (Exempted)
Female Candidates (All categories) NIL (Exempted)
Wards of serving/ex-JCOs/NCOs/ORs studying in Military Schools / Sainik Schools (run by Sainik Schools Society) NIL (Exempted - certificate from Principal required for verification at SSB)

Note: No fee exemption is available to OBC/EWS male candidates; they must pay the full prescribed fee.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Selection Process

Selection consists of a Written Examination conducted by UPSC, followed by Intelligence and Personality Test at the Services Selection Board (SSB) including CPSS for Air Force Flying branch, and finally Medical Examination. Success at the written examination confers no right of admission to the Academy. Final allocation is based on total marks secured in both Written Examination and SSB tests, subject to eligibility, medical fitness, and merit-cum-preference.

Stage 1 - Written Examination (Objective Type)

Paper Subject Code Duration Maximum Marks
Paper I Mathematics 01 2½ Hours 300
Paper II General Ability Test (Part A: English 200 marks + Part B: General Knowledge 400 marks) 02 2½ Hours 600
Total (Written)   900
SSB Test / Interview   900

Both papers are objective type only. Mathematics and Part B of General Ability Test are set bilingually in Hindi and English. Negative marking applies: 1/3 (0.33) marks deducted for each wrong answer. Calculators, mathematical instruments, and logarithmic tables are not permitted. Candidates must use Black Ball Pen only for all writing and marking in the OMR Answer Sheet. Face authentication is mandatory at the examination venue.

Stage 2 - SSB Intelligence & Personality Test

Candidates who qualify the written examination must register on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in (same email ID as given to UPSC) to be allotted their Selection Centre and SSB date. The SSB follows a two-stage process:

  • Stage I: Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) Tests and Picture Perception & Description Test (PP&DT). Only those who clear Stage I are admitted to Stage II.
  • Stage II: Interview, Group Testing Officer Tasks, Psychology Tests, and Conference - conducted over 4 days. Personality is assessed by the Interviewing Officer (IO), Group Testing Officer (GTO), and Psychologist, with equal weightage.

Candidates who qualify Stage II must submit original Matriculation certificate and 10+2 pass certificate or marks sheet for verification. Candidates who have opted for Air Force (Flying) shall undergo CPSS at the Selection Centre if willing. Candidates who fail CPSS or are in Habitual Wearing of Glasses (HWG) category can be considered for Air Force Ground Duty branches if willing. Travelling allowance (AC III Tier to-and-fro railway/bus fare) is paid to candidates appearing for SSB for the first time for a particular type of commission.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Candidates recommended by SSB undergo medical examination by a Board of Service Medical Officers. Only those declared fit are admitted to the Academy. Proceedings of the Medical Board are confidential. Candidates declared unfit will be intimated about the procedure for an Appeal Medical Board and, subsequently, a Review Medical Board. Candidates are advised to get themselves medically examined before applying to avoid disappointment at the final stage. Common defects found include wax in ears, deviated nasal septum, overweight/underweight, undersized chest, piles, tonsillitis, hydrocele/phimosis, gynaecomastia, and varicocele.

Tie-Breaking Principles:

If final marks (aggregate) are equal, the candidate securing more marks in written total is ranked higher. If still equal, by Paper II General Ability Test marks. If still equal, by age (senior ranked higher). Any remaining tie is resolved at the Commission's discretion. Marks are rounded off to two decimal digits at all stages.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Paper Subject / Part Key Topics
Paper I Mathematics (300 marks) Algebra, Matrices & Determinants, Trigonometry, Analytical Geometry (2D & 3D), Differential Calculus, Integral Calculus & Differential Equations, Vector Algebra, Statistics & Probability
Paper II – Part A English (200 marks) Grammar & usage, vocabulary, comprehension, cohesion in extended text
Paper II – Part B General Knowledge (400 marks) Physics (25%), Chemistry (15%), General Science (10%), History & Freedom Movement (20%), Geography (20%), Current Events (10%)

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Training & Career Details

Selected candidates undergo 3 years of combined academic and physical training at NDA, Khadakwasla, Pune (an Inter-Service institution). Army cadets pass out with B.Sc./B.Sc.(Computer)/B.A. from Jawaharlal Nehru University; Naval and Air Force cadets pass out with B.Tech degree (awarded at subsequent Pre-Commissioning Academies). Naval Academy (INAC) candidates undergo 4 years of training and are awarded B.Tech by JNU on successful completion.

After NDA, Army cadets proceed to IMA Dehradun (1 year; Permanent Commission as Lieutenant), Naval cadets to INA Ezhimala (1 year; Sub-Lieutenant), and Air Force Flying cadets to AFA Hyderabad (1.5 years flying training; Flying Officer on probation then confirmed). Air Force Ground Duty (Tech) cadets proceed to Air Force Technical College, Bengaluru; Ground Duty (Non-Tech) to AFA Hyderabad. Stipend during training: Rs. 56,100/- per month. Insurance cover: Rs. 30 lakh (Army Group Insurance Fund) from date of joining; Rs. 1.25 Cr cover during pre-commission training at NDA/IMA level on payment of monthly AGIF subscription of Rs. 12,500/-.

UPSC NDA 2 2026 - Exam Centres

The examination will be held across 86 cities in India. Allotment of centres (except Chennai, Dispur, Kolkata, and Nagpur) is on a "first-apply-first-allot" basis with a ceiling on candidates per centre. Once a centre reaches capacity it is frozen. Candidates are advised to apply early to secure their preferred centre. No request for change of centre will be entertained after allotment. Key centres include Agartala, Agra, Ahmedabad, Aizawl, Ajmer, Aligarh, Almora, Alwar, Bareilly, Bengaluru, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Bilaspur, Chandigarh, Chennai, Coimbatore, Cuttack, Dehradun, Delhi, Dharamshala, Dharwad, Dispur, Faridabad, Gangtok, Gaya, Ghaziabad, Gorakhpur, Gurugram, Gwalior, Hisar, Hyderabad, Imphal, Indore, Itanagar, Jabalpur, Jaipur, Jammu, Jodhpur, Jorhat, Kannur, Kanpur, Kargil, Kochi, Kohima, Kolkata, Kozhikode, Leh, Lucknow, Ludhiana, Madurai, Mumbai, Mysuru, Nagpur, Nasik, Navi Mumbai, Panaji, Patna, Port Blair, Prayagraj, Puducherry, Pune, Raipur, Rajkot, Ranchi, Sambalpur, Shillong, Shimla, Siliguri, Srinagar, Surat, Thane, Thiruvananthapuram, Tiruchirapalli, Tirupati, Udaipur, Varanasi, Vellore, Vijayawada, Visakhapatnam, and Warangal.

Competition Level Analysis for UPSC NDA 2 2026

The competition level for UPSC NDA & NA II 2026 is extremely high. With only 394 vacancies open to the entire country for one of the most prestigious defence academy entries, lakhs of Class 12 students and appearing candidates compete every year. The written exam covering Mathematics at Class 12 level and a 600-mark General Ability Test (Physics, Chemistry, Biology, History, Geography, Current Affairs, and English) demands thorough preparation across all subjects simultaneously.

The SSB stage is the most demanding filter - only candidates with strong officer-like qualities, communication ability, and mental fitness succeed here. For the Air Force Flying branch, additional CPSS qualification is a significant barrier. Female candidates have very limited vacancies (24 total across all wings and INAC) making competition in that segment especially intense. Candidates with strong Maths and Science backgrounds have an advantage, but General Knowledge and English sections are equally decisive for the final merit.

Who should NOT apply for UPSC NDA 2 2026?

  • Candidates who do not fall within the age band of born between 02-01-2008 and 01-01-2011 - there is no age relaxation of any kind for any category.
  • Married candidates - only unmarried male and female candidates are eligible. Marriage after application results in disqualification even if successful in the exam.
  • Candidates studying in Class 11; only Class 12 passed or appearing candidates are eligible. Class 11 students are explicitly not eligible.
  • Candidates applying for the Navy wing, Air Force wing, or INAC who do not have Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics at 10+2 level.
  • Candidates who have previously failed CPSS/PABT and wish to apply specifically for the Air Force Flying Branch (they may apply for Ground Duty branches).
  • Candidates who habitually wear glasses and are not willing to settle for Air Force Ground Duty branches instead of Flying.
  • Candidates who have been removed/withdrawn from any training academy of Armed Forces on disciplinary grounds or for lack of officer-like qualities.
  • Candidates debarred by the Ministry of Defence from holding any type of commission in the Defence Services.
  • Candidates with permanent body tattoos on any part of the body other than the inner forearm (inside elbow to wrist) or the back/dorsal of palm.
  • Candidates who cannot meet the physical fitness standards or have common medical defects (uncorrected deviated nasal septum, significant overweight/underweight, piles, varicocele, etc.) without prior rectification; they risk rejection at the medical stage after clearing written exam and SSB.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying UPSC NDA 2 2026?

  • Entering incorrect date of birth; once submitted to UPSC it cannot be changed at any stage or in any future examination under any circumstances.
  • Not checking subject eligibility before selecting Navy, Air Force, or INAC preferences; Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics at 10+2 level are mandatory for these wings.
  • Not registering on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in with the same email ID as provided to UPSC; this is mandatory after qualifying the written examination to receive SSB centre allotment and call-up information.
  • Uploading unclear, blurred, or incorrect photographs or signatures; signatures must be done three times (one below the other) on plain white paper in black ink.
  • Indicating service preferences carelessly; preferences cannot be changed after submission and candidates are considered only for services they have indicated.
  • Not keeping the registered email ID and mobile number active; all communications including e-Admit Card and SSB call-up are sent electronically.
  • Attempting to modify the application after submission; no correction, alteration, or modification is allowed in any field once submitted.
  • Bringing mobile phones, smart/digital watches, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic gadget to the examination venue; possession even in switched-off mode is grounds for disciplinary action and ban from future examinations.
  • Not entering the examination venue at least 30 minutes before commencement; entry is closed 30 minutes prior and no late entry is permitted under any circumstance.
  • Not carrying original Matriculation and 10+2 certificates to the SSB centre; candidates without originals will not be allowed to appear for SSB interview and will be sent back at their own expense.
  • Appearing candidates who fail to submit proof of passing 12th class by 10-06-2027; their candidature will be cancelled with no extension granted on any ground.

How to Apply for UPSC NDA 2 2026

Candidates must apply online only through https://upsconline.nic.in. No other mode of application is accepted. The Online Application Portal has four modules: Account Creation, Universal Registration (URN - one-time for lifetime), Common Application Form (CAF), and Examination Specific Module. The URN generated is common for all UPSC examinations; the Application Number generated is examination-specific and must be retained. Candidates in Government Service / Armed Forces / Sainik Schools / Military Schools must inform their Head of Office / Commanding Officer / Principal in writing that they have applied.

Documents / Details Required While Applying:

  • Date of Birth as per Matriculation / Secondary School Leaving Certificate
  • Educational qualification details (Class 12 pass certificate or appearing status)
  • Aadhaar Card (strongly advised as primary ID; other options: Voter Card/EPIC, PAN Card, Passport, Driving License, School Photo ID, or any Photo ID issued by State/Central Government)
  • Passport-size photograph (uploaded) and live photograph captured via webcam
  • Signature: signed three times (one below the other) on plain white paper with black ink, scanned and uploaded
  • URN (Universal Registration Number) - generate once, valid for all UPSC exams for lifetime
  • Fee payment: Rs. 100/- for General/OBC/EWS Male via Net Banking / Debit / Credit / RuPay / UPI; NIL for Female, SC/ST, and Wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs studying in Military/Sainik Schools
  • Service preferences (1 to 4) to be indicated carefully in the Examination Specific Module

Documents to be Carried to SSB / Kept Ready After Written Result:

  • Original Matriculation / Secondary School Examination Certificate showing date of birth
  • Original 10+2 pass certificate or marks sheet (for candidates who have passed); bonafide certificate from school/college (for appearing candidates)
  • Fee exemption certificate from Principal (for Wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs studying in Military/Sainik Schools)
  • NOC from Commanding Officer (for Armed Forces / Naval Sailors candidates)
  • Any other documents as required by the respective Services HQ

Apply Online at: https://upsconline.nic.in

For queries: Call 011-24041001 / 011-40303444 (10:00 AM to 5:30 PM, working days, 20-05-2026 to 09-06-2026) or email [email protected]. All communications must include Name, Course Number & Year of Exam, URN, Application Number, Roll Number (if received), full name in block letters, complete postal address, and registered email ID / mobile number.

After clearing the written exam: For Army (first choice) - visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. For Navy (first choice) - visit www.joinindiannavy.gov.in or email [email protected]. For Air Force (first choice) - register on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in for AFSB and date selection.

Important Instructions for UPSC NDA 2 2026 Candidates

  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn. No correction/alteration/modification in any field of the application is allowed after submission under any circumstances.
  • The URN Profile provides a one-time edit facility; changes to the URN profile do not reflect in applications already submitted and apply only to future applications.
  • Candidates must indicate service preferences carefully (1 to 4) in the Examination Specific Module; no addition/alteration is entertained after submission. It is mandatory to exercise at least one preference.
  • All candidates who qualify the written examination must register on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in with the same email ID as provided to UPSC to receive SSB centre allotment. Those already registered should not register again.
  • e-Admit Cards are available only on the Commission's website for download. No admit card is sent by post or email. Candidates must enter the venue at least 30 minutes before commencement; entry is closed 30 minutes prior.
  • Face authentication is mandatory at the examination venue for all candidates.
  • Use Black Ball Pen only for all writing/marking in OMR sheets. Any omission/mistake in encoding Roll Number or Test Booklet Series Code will render the answer sheet liable for rejection.
  • Negative marking applies: 1/3rd of marks deducted for each wrong answer. Marking more than one answer is also treated as wrong. Questions left blank carry no penalty.
  • Mobile phones, smart/digital watches, pagers, Bluetooth devices, calculators, and any electronic equipment are strictly banned inside the examination premises even in switched-off mode. Infringement entails disciplinary action including permanent/specified-period ban from UPSC examinations and possible prosecution under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024.
  • Candidates must undertake not to marry until they complete their full training. Marriage during training leads to discharge and recovery of all Government expenditure.
  • Candidates appear before SSB at their own risk and are not entitled to compensation for any injury sustained during SSB tests. Parents/guardians must sign a certificate to this effect.
  • Candidates who fail to report to the Academy within 7 days of the specified reporting date without prior written intimation will have their candidature liable for cancellation.
  • Candidates previously removed from NDA/INAC for lack of officer-like qualities or on disciplinary grounds are not eligible. Those withdrawn on medical grounds or who left voluntarily remain eligible, provided they satisfy medical and other prescribed conditions.
  • Representations on question papers and provisional answer keys can be submitted only through the Commission's website within 5 days of the examination (Day 3 to Day 7 up to 6:00 PM). No representation by email/post/hand or any other mode is accepted.
  • Government strives to have a workforce that reflects gender balance; women candidates are encouraged to apply.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the UPSC website only. In case of any dispute between English and Hindi versions of the notification, the English version will prevail.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 Notification PDF

Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has officially released Examination Notice No. 11/2026-CDS-II dated 20/05/2026 for the Combined Defence Services Examination (II), 2026 to be held on 13.09.2026. The notification includes complete details on course-wise vacancies, eligibility conditions, scheme of examination, syllabus, selection process, exam centres, application procedure, and service conditions for each academy. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official UPSC website.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 Overview

UPSC is conducting CDS Examination II, 2026 for 451 vacancies across IMA, INA, AFA, and OTA courses, with last date 09 June 2026.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 451 vacancies have been announced across 5 courses. The number of vacancies is tentative and may be changed at any stage of the selection process by Services HQ. NCC 'C' Certificate holders have reserved vacancies in IMA, INA, and AFA. Women candidates are eligible only for the OTA SSC Women (NT) course.

S. No. Course Academy Commencement Total Vacancies
1 163rd (DE) Course Indian Military Academy (IMA), Dehradun July 2027 100
2 Executive Branch (General Service) / Hydro Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala July 2027 26

Left-over finally qualified IMA/INA/AFA candidates who could not be inducted due to lack of vacancies may be considered for SSC(NT) even without indicating that preference, subject to conditions.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions before the date of application. Their admission at all stages is purely provisional subject to satisfying eligibility conditions. Verification of eligibility with reference to original documents will be taken up only after the candidate qualifies for SSB/Personality Test.

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal / Person of Indian origin migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia) or Vietnam - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable. Gorkha subjects of Nepal do not need such certificate.
Educational Qualification – IMA & OTA Degree of a recognised University or equivalent.
Educational Qualification – Indian Naval Academy Degree in Engineering OR B.Sc. with Physics as core/elective subject from a recognised University (with Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level).
Educational Qualification – Air Force Academy Degree of a recognised University with Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level, OR Bachelor of Engineering.
Final Year Students Candidates in the final year/semester of their Degree course may apply but must submit proof of passing the degree examination by the prescribed date (IMA/INA by 01-07-2027; AFA by 13-05-2027; OTA by 01-10-2027). A bonafide certificate from College/University is required at the time of SSB interview.
Physical Standards Candidates must be physically fit as per the "Joint Order Manual of Medical Standards for Entry into Armed Forces (1st Edition)" available at www.joinindianarmy.nic.in. Permanent body tattoos allowed only on inner forearm (inside elbow to wrist) and back of palm; not permitted on any other body part.
Debarment Candidates debarred by the Ministry of Defence from holding any type of commission in the Defence Services are not eligible.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 Application Form Date

The online application process for UPSC CDS 2 2026 has officially started on 20 May 2026 and will remain open until 09 June 2026 (up to 6:00 PM). Candidates must submit their applications through the official UPSC online portal within this deadline, as no extensions are typically provided.

Applicants are advised to complete the form well before the last date to avoid last-minute server issues. The application process includes registration, filling in personal and educational details, uploading required documents, and payment of the application fee (if applicable).

  • Application Start Date: 20 May 2026

  • Last Date to Apply: 09 June 2026 (6:00 PM)

  • Mode of Application: Online only

  • Official Website: https://upsconline.nic.in

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Age Limit & Marital Status

Age is calculated based on date of birth as recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate only. No other document (horoscope, affidavit, birth extract, etc.) will be accepted. Once a date of birth is submitted, no change will be allowed subsequently.

Course Age Limit Gender & Marital Status Date of Birth Range
Indian Military Academy (IMA) 19 to 24 years Unmarried Male only Born between 02-07-2003 and 01-07-2008 (both inclusive)
Indian Naval Academy (INA) 19 to 24 years Unmarried Male only Born between 02-07-2003 and 01-07-2008 (both inclusive)
Air Force Academy (AFA) 20 to 24 years (as on 01-07-2027) Male; below 25 years must be unmarried; married candidates aged 25+ eligible but no married accommodation during training Born between 02-07-2003 and 01-07-2007; CPL holders: up to 26 years (born not earlier than 01-07-2001)

Note: Male divorcees/widowers cannot be treated as unmarried male and are not eligible for IMA/INA/AFA/OTA courses. Marriage is not permitted during training at any academy; a candidate who marries after applying will not be selected and a candidate marrying during training shall be discharged and liable to refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Application Fee

Fee is payable through online mode only (Net Banking / Visa / Master / Rupay Credit / Debit Card / UPI). Payment through any other mode is not valid. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination.

Category Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male) Rs. 200/- (Non-Refundable)
Female Candidates (All categories) NIL (Exempted)
SC / ST Candidates NIL (Exempted)

Note: No fee exemption is available to OBC/EWS male candidates; they must pay the full prescribed fee.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Selection Process

Selection consists of a Written Examination conducted by UPSC followed by Intelligence and Personality Test at the Services Selection Board (SSB), and finally Medical Examination. Success at the written examination confers no right of admission to any academy; final selection is made in order of merit subject to medical fitness and number of vacancies available.

Stage 1 - Written Examination (Objective Type)

For IMA, INA, and Air Force Academy:

Sl. Subject Code Duration Maximum Marks
1 English 11 2 Hours 100
2 General Knowledge 12 2 Hours 100
3 Elementary Mathematics 13 2 Hours 100
Total (Written)   300
SSB / Interview Marks   300

For Officers' Training Academy (OTA):

Sl. Subject Code Duration Maximum Marks
1 English 11 2 Hours 100
2 General Knowledge 12 2 Hours 100
Total (Written)   200
SSB / Interview Marks   200

All papers are objective type only. General Knowledge and Elementary Mathematics papers are set bilingually in Hindi and English. Negative marking applies: 1/3 (0.33) marks deducted for each wrong answer. Calculators are not permitted. Candidates must use Black Ball Pen only for writing and marking in OMR Answer Sheet. Face authentication is mandatory at the examination venue.

Stage 2 - SSB Intelligence & Personality Test

Candidates who qualify the written examination will be called for SSB test by the respective Services HQ based on preference. The SSB follows a two-stage process:

  • Stage I: Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) Tests and Picture Perception & Description Test (PP&DT). Only those who clear Stage I are admitted to Stage II.
  • Stage II: Interview, Group Testing Officer Tasks, Psychology Tests, and Conference - conducted over 4 days. The personality is assessed by the Interviewing Officer (IO), Group Testing Officer (GTO), and Psychologist, with equal weightage.

Candidates who qualify for IMA/INA/AFA (irrespective of OTA qualification) will be detailed for SSB in February–March 2027. Candidates who qualify for SSC (OTA) only will be detailed for SSB in April–June 2027. Every AFA candidate is given CPSS/PABT only once - the grade secured at first test holds good for every subsequent interview. Candidates who have previously failed CPSS and those who habitually wear spectacles are not eligible for Air Force.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Final selection is subject to the candidate being declared medically fit as per the "Joint Order Manual of Medical Standards for Entry into Armed Forces (1st Edition)". Medical standards and procedures are available at www.joinindianarmy.nic.in.

Tie-Breaking Principles:

For IMA/INA/AFA: candidates equal in aggregate (Final Marks) are ranked by written total; if still equal, by GK paper marks; if still equal, by age (senior ranked higher). For OTA: same process but written total is English + GK only. Any remaining tie is resolved at the Commission's discretion.

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Subject Standard Key Topics
English Graduate level Understanding of English and workmanlike use of words
General Knowledge Graduate level Current events, everyday science, History of India, Geography
Elementary Mathematics Matriculation (Class X) Arithmetic (Number System, fractions, percentages, ratio, S.I./C.I.), Algebra (polynomials, quadratic equations, simultaneous equations), Trigonometry, Geometry, Mensuration, Statistics

UPSC CDS 2 2026 - Exam Centres

The examination will be held across 84 cities in India. Allotment of centres (except Chennai, Dispur, Kolkata, and Nagpur) is on a "first-apply-first-allot" basis with a ceiling on candidates per centre. Once a centre reaches capacity, it is frozen. Candidates are advised to apply early to secure their preferred centre. No request for change of examination centre will be entertained after allotment. Key centres include Agartala, Agra, Ahmedabad, Aizawl, Ajmer, Aligarh, Almora, Alwar, Bareilly, Bengaluru, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Chandigarh, Chennai, Coimbatore, Dehradun, Delhi, Gangtok, Hyderabad, Imphal, Itanagar, Jaipur, Jammu, Jorhat, Kanpur, Kargil, Kochi, Kohima, Kolkata, Leh, Lucknow, Ludhiana, Mumbai, Mysuru, Nagpur, Patna, Prayagraj, Pune, Raipur, Rajkot, Ranchi, Shillong, Shimla, Siliguri, Srinagar, Surat, Thiruvananthapuram, Tiruchirapalli, Tirupati, Udaipur, Varanasi, Vijayawada, Visakhapatnam, and others.

Competition Level Analysis for UPSC CDS 2 2026

The competition level for UPSC CDS II 2026 is expected to be very high. With only 451 vacancies - of which 275 are for OTA SSC Men (NT) alone - the exam is among the most competitive defence officer entry examinations in India. Lakhs of graduates appear for CDS every year, and the three-stage selection process (written exam, SSB, and medical) filters candidates rigorously at each stage.

The written exam is of moderate to high difficulty at the graduate/matriculate level but negative marking makes accuracy critical. The SSB stage is the most challenging differentiator - only candidates with genuine officer-like qualities, communication skills, and psychological fitness progress. AFA vacancies (32 seats) are the most competitive due to additional CPSS/PABT screening. Candidates already registered on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in must ensure the same email ID is used for UPSC registration.

Who should NOT apply for UPSC CDS 2 2026?

  • Candidates who do not hold or are not in the final year of a Degree programme from a recognised University - there is no provision for diploma or 10+2-only candidates.
  • Candidates outside the age band for their chosen course - even a single day's deviation makes the candidate ineligible, with no relaxation available (unlike civil service exams).
  • Married male candidates applying for IMA, INA, or AFA; and married male candidates under 25 years applying for AFA.
  • Male divorcees and widowers; they cannot be treated as unmarried and are not eligible for any of the courses.
  • Women candidates who wish to join IMA, INA, or AFA - women are eligible only for OTA SSC Women (NT) course.
  • Candidates for INA who do not have Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level and a B.Sc. with Physics or an Engineering degree.
  • Candidates for AFA who do not have Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level or an Engineering degree; also those who habitually wear spectacles or have previously failed CPSS.
  • Candidates who have been removed from any training academy (IMA/INA/AFA/NDA/OTA) on disciplinary grounds or for lack of officer-like qualities.
  • Candidates debarred by the Ministry of Defence from holding any type of commission in the Defence Services.
  • Candidates unable to meet the physical fitness standards required for reporting to IMA or OTA (e.g. 2.4 km run in 10:30 min for men, push-ups, pull-ups, sit-ups as specified).
  • Candidates with permanent body tattoos anywhere on the body other than the inner forearm (inside elbow to wrist) or the back/dorsal of palm.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying UPSC CDS 2 2026?

  • Entering incorrect date of birth; once submitted to UPSC it cannot be changed at any stage or in any future examination under any circumstances.
  • Indicating wrong course preferences or not placing AFA as first choice when competing for the Air Force Academy - AFA as second/third choice is treated as invalid.
  • Male candidates placing OTA at a higher preference than IMA/INA/AFA; OTA must always be the last preference, otherwise it is automatically treated as last regardless.
  • Not registering on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in before SSB results if Army (IMA/OTA) is the first choice - this is mandatory to receive SSB call-up information.
  • Using a different email ID for UPSC and for the DG Recruiting website; both must be identical and unique to the applicant.
  • Uploading unclear, blurred, or incorrect photographs or signatures; signatures must be done three times (one below the other) on plain white paper in black ink.
  • Not carrying original Matriculation certificate and Degree/Provisional Degree certificate to the SSB centre; candidates without originals are sent back at their own expense.
  • Attempting to modify the application after submission; no corrections, alterations, or modifications are allowed in any field once the application is submitted.
  • Bringing mobile phones, smart/digital watches, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic gadget to the examination venue; possession even in switched-off mode is grounds for disciplinary action and ban from future examinations.
  • Not entering the examination venue at least 30 minutes before commencement; entry is closed 30 minutes prior and no late entry is permitted under any circumstances.

How to Apply for UPSC CDS 2 2026

Candidates must apply online only through https://upsconline.nic.in. No other mode of application is accepted. The Online Application Portal has four modules: Account Creation, Universal Registration (URN - one-time for lifetime), Common Application Form (CAF), and Examination Specific Module. The URN generated is common for all UPSC examinations; the Application Number generated is examination-specific and must be retained.

Candidates in Government Service / Armed Forces must inform their Head of Office / Commanding Officer in writing that they have applied. Armed Forces candidates must also submit NOC at the time of SSB interview. Candidates should also give their consent for the Pratibha Setu Portal (Public Disclosure Scheme) at the time of filling the Examination Specific Module if they wish their details to be shared with registered organisations for employment purposes after final result.

Documents / Details Required While Applying:

  • Date of Birth as per Matriculation / Secondary School Examination Certificate
  • Educational qualification details and supporting documents
  • Aadhaar Card (strongly advised as primary ID; other options: Voter Card/EPIC, PAN Card, Passport, Driving License, or any Photo ID issued by State/Central Government)
  • Passport-size photograph (uploaded) and live photograph captured via webcam
  • Signature: signed three times (one below the other) on plain white paper with black ink, scanned and uploaded
  • URN (Universal Registration Number) - generate once, valid for all UPSC exams for lifetime
  • Fee payment via Net Banking / Debit / Credit / Rupay / UPI (Rs. 200/- for General/OBC/EWS Male; NIL for Female and SC/ST)

Documents to be Kept Ready after Written Result (for SSB):

  • Original Matriculation / Secondary School Examination Certificate showing date of birth
  • Original Degree / Provisional Degree Certificate and mark sheets of all years/semesters
  • Final year students: Bonafide certificate from Principal/College stating candidate will pass degree exam by specified date
  • NCC 'C' Certificate (Army / Naval / Air Wing) for candidates applying under NCC reserved vacancies, to be submitted by 13-05-2027
  • NOC from Commanding Officer (for Armed Forces candidates) to be submitted at SSB

Apply Online at: https://upsconline.nic.in

For queries: Call 011-24041001 / 011-40303444 (10:00 AM to 5:30 PM, working days, 20-05-2026 to 09-06-2026) or email [email protected]. All communications must include Name & Year of Exam, URN, Application Number, Roll Number (if received), full name, complete postal address, and registered email ID / mobile number.

Important Instructions for UPSC CDS 2 2026 Candidates

  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn. No correction/alteration/modification in any field of the application is allowed after submission under any circumstances.
  • The URN Profile provides a one-time edit facility; changes to URN profile do not reflect in applications already submitted and apply only to future applications.
  • Candidates must indicate course preferences carefully; no addition/alteration in preferences is entertained after submission. When first choice is offered and a candidate declines, candidature is cancelled for all other choices for regular commission.
  • AFA must compulsorily be indicated as first choice if the candidate wishes to join the Air Force Academy; choice for AFA as second/third etc. is invalid.
  • OTA must always be the last preference for male candidates competing for both Permanent Commission (IMA/INA/AFA) and Short Service Commission (OTA) courses.
  • e-Admit Cards are available only on the Commission's website for download. No admit card is sent by post or email. Candidates must enter the venue at least 30 minutes before commencement; entry is closed 30 minutes prior.
  • Face authentication is mandatory at the examination venue for all candidates.
  • Use Black Ball Pen only for all writing/marking in OMR sheets. Any omission/mistake in encoding Roll Number or Test Booklet Series Code will render the answer sheet liable for rejection.
  • Negative marking applies: 1/3rd of marks deducted for each wrong answer. Marking more than one answer is also treated as wrong.
  • Mobile phones, smart/digital watches, pagers, Bluetooth devices, calculators, and any electronic equipment are strictly banned inside the examination premises even in switched-off mode. Infringement entails disciplinary action including permanent/specified-period ban from UPSC examinations.
  • Candidates who qualify at the written exam and have Army (IMA/OTA) as first choice must register on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in to receive SSB call-up information. The email ID used there must be the same as registered with UPSC.
  • Candidates will appear for SSB at their own risk and are not entitled to compensation for any injury sustained during SSB tests.
  • Candidates who were previously removed from any Service Academy on disciplinary grounds or for lack of officer-like qualities are not eligible to apply.
  • Marriage subsequent to the date of application will disqualify the candidate even if successful in the examination. A candidate who marries during training will be discharged and must refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.
  • After admission to IMA / INA / AFA, candidates will not be considered for any other commission and will not be permitted to appear for any other interview or examination.
  • Representations on question papers and provisional answer keys can be submitted only through the Commission's website within 5 days of the examination (Day 3 to Day 7 up to 6:00 PM). No representation by email/post/hand or any other mode is accepted.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the UPSC website only - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Government strives to have a workforce that reflects gender balance; women candidates are encouraged to apply.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Professor (Teaching Faculty – Medical Specialties) posts! Published on 19 May 2026 at www.esic.gov.in, this new recruitment drive offers 118 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the age group of up to 50 years (relaxations as applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/ESIC employees) and possessing the required medical or postgraduate qualifications can apply offline via the prescribed application form from now until 27 July 2026 (03 August 2026 for remote area candidates). Find all details about the ESIC Professor new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, application procedure, and notification PDF here

FOLLOW US

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The detailed notification titled “Recruitment of Teaching Faculty (Professors) in ESIC PGIMSRs and ESIC Medical Colleges on Direct Recruitment Basis” has been released by ESIC Headquarters, New Delhi. The notification provides complete information about specialty-wise vacancies, identified posts for PwBD, age limit, pay scale, educational qualifications, experience requirements, selection procedure, application fee, concessions/relaxations and the prescribed application form. Candidates must read the notification thoroughly before applying to ensure they meet all eligibility conditions.

  • Download ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC)
Administrative Ministry Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India
Post Name Professor (Teaching Faculty – Medical Specialties)
Mode of Recruitment Direct Recruitment (Interview‑based)
Total Vacancies 118 Professor posts across various specialties
Pay Scale Pay Level‑13 in Pay Matrix (Rs. 1,23,100 – 2,15,900) plus Non‑Practicing Allowance (NPA) and other admissible allowances
Job Location ESIC PGIMSRs and ESIC Medical Colleges across various States; liable to posting anywhere in India
Application Mode Offline – via prescribed application form sent by Speed Post/file:2
Selection Mode Interview by ESIC Selection Board; shortlisting based on experience if applications are large
Official Website https://www.esic.gov.in

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Vacancy Details

ESIC proposes to fill a total of 118 Professor posts in various specialties with category‑wise breakup as given below. Reserved vacancies include backlog vacancies of 11 SC, 6 ST and 23 OBC posts, and 8 posts are reserved for PwBD under categories (a), (b), (c) and (d & e) as per DoPT guidelines.

S. No. Specialty Total
1 Anatomy 7
2 Anesthesiology 7
3 Biochemistry 4
4 Community Medicine 7
5 Dermatology 1
6 Emergency Medicine 6
7 Forensic Medicine & Toxicology (FMT) 5
8 General Medicine 8
9 General Surgery 8
10 Microbiology 7
11 Ophthalmology (Eye) 4
12 Orthopedics 8
13 Otorhinolaryngology (ENT) 2
14 Pediatrics 6
15 Pathology 6
16 Pharmacology 5
17 Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation 2
18 Physiology 5
19 Psychiatry 3
20 Radiodiagnosis (Radiology) 4
21 Respiratory Medicine 4
22 Transfusion Medicine (Blood Bank) 9
  Total 118

Out of the total, 8 posts are reserved for PwBD across categories (a) – blindness and low vision, (b) – deaf and hard of hearing, (c) – locomotor disability and related conditions, and (d & e) – autism, intellectual disability, specific learning disability, mental illness and multiple disabilities.

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Notification 19 May 2026
Last Date for Receipt of Application (All India) 27 July 2026
Last Date for Remote Area Candidates 03 August 2026 (for candidates from NE States, J&K Ladakh division, specified HP districts, Andaman & Nicobar, Lakshadweep etc.)

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure that they satisfy all eligibility conditions relating to nationality, age, educational qualifications and teaching experience as of the closing date of receipt of applications.

Criteria Details
Nationality / Citizenship Candidate must be a citizen of India; certain categories of Nepal, Bhutan, Tibetan refugees and PIO from specified countries may also apply, subject to Government of India eligibility certificate.
Age Limit (UR / EWS) Not exceeding 50 years for Professor as on closing date of applications (27.07.2026 for most candidates).
Age Relaxation Up to 5 years for ESIC / Government servants; 5 years for SC/ST; 3 years for OBC; 10 years for PwBD (with cumulative maximum as per rules); additional relaxations for Ex‑Servicemen as per Government orders.
Educational Qualification – Medical Candidates
  • A recognised medical qualification included in First or Second Schedule or Part‑II of Third Schedule to IMC Act 1956, fulfilling Section 13(3) conditions where applicable.
  • Postgraduate qualification – MD/MS or equivalent in the concerned subject or allied discipline.
Educational Qualification – Non‑Medical Candidates Postgraduate degree (Master’s) in concerned subject/allied discipline and a Doctorate degree (PhD) from a recognised University in the respective subject/allied discipline, as per MCI/NMC “Minimum Qualifications for Teachers in Medical Institutions Regulations”.
Teaching Experience
  • Four years’ teaching experience as Reader/Associate Professor in a recognised medical college in the concerned specialty; OR
  • Three years’ teaching experience as Associate Professor in the concerned specialty, with at least four research publications in indexed/national journals as first author, of which minimum two are during Associate Professor tenure.
Experience Recognition Teaching experience must be recognised by Medical Council of India/National Medical Commission or relevant statutory body; experience as General Duty Medical Officer is not counted.
PwBD Eligibility Only persons with minimum 40% benchmark disability in notified categories are eligible for PwBD reservation and age relaxation; only disabilities identified as suitable for each specialty as per the notification will be considered.

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Pay Scale & Emoluments

Selected candidates will be appointed in Pay Level‑13 of the 7th CPC pay matrix with Non‑Practicing Allowance and other admissible benefits.

  • Basic Pay: Level‑13 (Rs. 1,23,100 – 2,15,900) in the pay matrix.
  • Allowances: Dearness Allowance (DA), Non‑Practicing Allowance (NPA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance and other allowances as per Central Government/ESIC rules.
  • Career Prospects: Promotional avenues available under DACP guidelines; opportunities for academic and research growth within ESIC medical institutions.

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Application Fee

Application fee is payable via Demand Draft/Banker’s Cheque only, in favour of “ESI Fund Account No. 2” payable at Delhi.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC / EWS / ESIC & Departmental Male Candidates Rs. 500/- (non‑refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD / Women (all categories) / Ex‑Servicemen No Fee

Fee once paid will not be refunded in any circumstances and applications without the prescribed fee (where applicable) will be rejected without correspondence.

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Selection Process

Selection will be made on the basis of an Interview conducted by the ESIC Selection Board, with possible shortlisting based on experience.

  • If the number of applications is large, ESIC may shortlist candidates for interview based on teaching experience beyond the minimum required level (Associate Professor/Reader or above) as per Recruitment Rules.
  • Category‑wise minimum qualifying marks in interview: UR/EWS – 50, OBC – 45, SC/ST/PwBD – 40 out of 100 marks.
  • Tie‑breaking: older candidate by age is preferred; if still tied, earlier date of passing essential PG degree; if still tied, earlier date of completion of internship is considered.
  • A Reserve List may be maintained as per UPSC‑like provisions.

ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026 – Age Relaxation & Reservation Notes

ESIC follows Government of India rules on age relaxation, community reservation and PwBD concessions.

  • SC/ST – up to 5 years; OBC – up to 3 years; PwBD – up to 10 years (maximum age on closing date not exceeding 56 years), with cumulative relaxation for SC/ST/OBC PwBD as per norms.
  • Ex‑Servicemen and ECOs/SSCOs – age relaxation up to 5 years subject to conditions on length of service and release certificates.
  • Only candidates belonging to castes/communities in the Central List of SC/ST/OBC and having valid, current certificates in the prescribed format will get reservation benefits; multiple applications in different categories will lead to rejection.

How to Apply – ESIC Professor Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted offline only in the prescribed application form attached with the notification. No online application facility is available for this recruitment.

Step‑by‑Step Offline Application Process:

  1. Download and print the official notification and Application Form for “Professor in ESI Corporation‑2026” from ESIC website or your job portal.
  2. Fill the application form in BLOCK letters with accurate details such as specialty applied for, personal information, educational qualifications, teaching experience, registration details and publication list.
  3. Prepare a Demand Draft/Banker’s Cheque of Rs. 500/- (if applicable) in favour of “ESI Fund Account No. 2”, payable at Delhi, issued after the date of advertisement.
  4. Attach self‑attested copies of required documents (educational certificates, experience certificates, caste/EWS/PwBD certificates, proof of age, research publications, etc.) as per the checklist.
  5. Affix a recent self‑attested passport size photograph in the space provided on the application form.
  6. Place the filled and signed application form along with DD/Banker’s Cheque and enclosures in an envelope superscribed “Application for the post of Professor for Medical Institutions”.
  7. Send the application preferably by Speed Post to the address given below so as to reach on or before 27.07.2026 (03.08.2026 for specified remote areas).

Application Sending Address:

Postal Address Regional Office Delhi, ESI Corporation, DDA Complex‑cum‑Office, 3rd & 4th Floor, Rajendra Bhawan, Rajendra Place, New Delhi – 110008
DD/Banker’s Cheque Payable at Delhi

Applications received after the last date or without proper documents/fees will be rejected, and ESIC will not be responsible for postal delays.

Documents Required with ESIC Professor Application

Candidates must attach self‑attested copies of all relevant documents along with the application form.

  • Original Demand Draft/Banker’s Cheque pinned on top of the application form with candidate’s name and address written on the reverse.
  • Recent passport size photograph (self‑attested) firmly pasted on the application form.
  • Matriculation/10th certificate or mark sheet indicating Date of Birth; in some cases, school leaving certificate for TN & Kerala.
  • Degree certificates and mark sheets of all academic years (MBBS, PG, PhD etc.); provisional certificates accepted if final degree is awaited.
  • Experience certificates from Heads of Organisations clearly mentioning post, period, pay and nature of duties, and specifying recognition as teaching experience by MCI/NMC or relevant body.
  • Certificates/testimonials supporting research publications and academic achievements.
  • Category certificates: SC/ST/OBC/EWS in prescribed Central Government format; OBC non‑creamy layer declaration; EWS Income & Asset certificate.
  • PwBD certificate in prescribed format for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities.
  • Documents for age relaxation (Ex‑Servicemen, Central/UT Govt employees etc.) in the prescribed formats.
  • Any other supporting documents for claims made in the application (name change, etc.).

Important Instructions for ESIC Professor Applicants

  • Eligibility (age, qualifications, experience etc.) is calculated with reference to the closing date of applications – 27.07.2026 for most candidates.
  • Mere submission of application or call for interview does not confer any right to appointment; candidature remains provisional until verification.
  • Incomplete/unsigned applications, or those not in prescribed proforma, without required documents, fee, or received after the last date will be rejected without communication.
  • Any false statement or suppression of material information can lead to cancellation of candidature/appointment at any stage.
  • Selected candidates will be on probation as per ESIC rules and are liable to be posted anywhere in India; state preferences are only indicative and not binding.
