  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Jobs 2026: OnlyJobs provides all active vacancies for Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Ranger, Field Guard and higher posts like Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) and Indian Forest Service officers across India. Here you can find state-wise forest department notifications with vacancy, qualification, eligibility, selection process, salary and application last date in one place.

Latest Forest Department Jobs 2026

Below is the updated list of ongoing forest department recruitment notifications, including posts such as Forest Guard (Van Rakshak), Forester, Range Forest Officer (RFO), Field Guard and other technical and non-technical vacancies. Click on ‘View Details’ to read the full notification and apply online/offline as per instructions.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Post Pay Level Basic Pay Gross Salary (Approx) Category
Forest Guard / Van Rakshak / Vana Palaka Level 3 ₹21,700 ₹28,000 – ₹35,000 Group C
Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B
Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF) Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹75,000 – ₹95,000 Group B
Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) Level 12 ₹78,800 ₹1,00,000 – ₹1,20,000 Group A
Conservator of Forests (CF) Level 13 ₹1,23,100 ₹1,50,000 – ₹1,80,000 Group A
Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF) Level 14 ₹1,44,200 ₹1,80,000 – ₹2,10,000 IFS Cadre
Additional PCCF (Addl. PCCF) Level 15 ₹1,82,200 ₹2,10,000 – ₹2,20,000 IFS Cadre
Principal Chief Conservator (PCCF / HoFF) Level 17 ₹2,25,000 ₹2,25,000 (fixed) Apex Scale

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

📅 Updated: 21 May 2026 ✍️ By: OnlyJobs Editorial Team 🌐 Source: Official Notification

Check complete details for Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Online Form 2026. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

⚡ Key Highlights

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Indian Air Force has released the latest AFCAT 02/2026 notification for recruitment of Men and Women as Group ‘A’ Gazetted Officers in the Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non‑Technical) branches for courses commencing in July 2027. Online applications will be accepted from 20 May 2026 (1100 hrs) to 19 June 2026 (2330 hrs) through the official AFCAT website https://afcat.edcil.co.in, and the online AFCAT exam will be conducted on 08 August 2026 (Saturday). Interested and eligible candidates must ensure that they fulfill all eligibility conditions before applying.

FOLLOW US

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Notification PDF

Indian Air Force has published the detailed notification titled “AIR FORCE COMMON ADMISSION TEST (AFCAT‑02/2026) FOR FLYING BRANCH AND GROUND DUTY (TECHNICAL AND NON‑TECHNICAL) BRANCHES / NCC SPECIAL ENTRY / GATE SCORE ENTRY FOR COURSES COMMENCING IN JULY 2027”. The notification includes complete information on branches, course numbers, vacancies, eligibility, age limits, educational qualifications, selection process, exam pattern, medical standards, training, and how to apply. Candidates must read the full notification carefully before submitting the online application form.

  • Download AFCAT 02/2026 Official Notification PDF

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Overview

Organization Name Indian Air Force (IAF)
Exam Name Air Force Common Admission Test (AFCAT‑02/2026)
Course Commencement July 2027 (at Air Force Training Establishments)
Branches Flying Branch, Ground Duty (Technical), Ground Duty (Non‑Technical)
Entry Types AFCAT Entry, NCC Special Entry (Flying), GATE Score Entry (Ground Duty Technical)
Application Mode Online only via AFCAT website (https://afcat.edcil.co.in)
Exam Mode Online Computer Based Test (CBT) - AFCAT
Course Type Group ‘A’ Gazetted Officer training leading to Permanent/Short Service Commission.
Commission Types Permanent Commission (PC) and Short Service Commission (SSC) for Men and Women (as applicable).
Online Examination Date 08 August 2026 (Saturday)
Official AFCAT Website https://afcat.edcil.co.in

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Opening Date for Online Applications 20 May 2026 (1100 hrs)
Closing Date for Online Applications 19 June 2026 (2330 hrs)
AFCAT Online Examination Date 08 August 2026 (Saturday)
AFCAT Admit Card Download From 04 August 2026 (1100 hrs) onwards (tentative)
Course Commencement July 2027

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Entry, Branch & Vacancies

AFCAT 02/2026 notification gives branch‑wise and entry‑wise vacancies, which are tentative and subject to change as per organisational requirements. Two seats in Ground Duty (Non‑Technical) are reserved for Law‑qualified candidates.

Entry Branch Vacancies - Men (PC) Vacancies - Women (PC) Vacancies - Men (SSC) Vacancies - Women (SSC)
AFCAT Entry Flying - - 30 5
AFCAT Entry Ground Duty (Technical) Aeronautical Engineer (Electronics) [AE(L)] & Aeronautical Engineer (Mechanical) [AE(M)] AE(L): 2 AE(M): 8 AE(L): 2 AE(M): 2 - -
- - AE(L): 107 AE(M): 52 AE(L): 25 AE(M): 13
AFCAT Entry Ground Duty (Non‑Technical) Weapon Systems (WS), Administration, Logistics, Accounts, Education, Meteorology - - WS: 21, Adm: 50, Lgs: 4, Accts: 5, Edn: 1, Met: 1 WS: 5, Adm: 12, Lgs: 2, Accts: 2, Edn: 2, Met: 2
NCC Special Entry Flying 10% seats out of CDSE vacancies for PC and 10% seats out of AFCAT vacancies for SSC reserved for NCC Air Wing ‘C’ Certificate holders.
GATE Score Entry Ground Duty (Technical) AE(L) & AE(M) AE(L): 1 AE(M): 1 AE(L): 1 AE(M): 1 AE(L): 12 AE(M): 6 AE(L): 3 AE(M): 1

*Vacancies are indicative and may change without notice depending on training slots and organisational requirements.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure that they satisfy all eligibility conditions of nationality, age, marital status, educational qualification and medical standards as on the prescribed dates. Their admission at all stages is purely provisional and will be cancelled if any ineligibility is detected at any stage.

Criteria Details
Nationality Candidate must be a citizen of India as per Indian Citizenship Act, 1955.
Age - Flying Branch (AFCAT & NCC Special Entry) 20 to 24 years as on 01 July 2027 i.e. born between 02 July 2003 and 01 July 2007 (both dates inclusive). Upper age limit relaxable up to 26 years (born between 02 July 2001 and 01 July 2007) for candidates holding valid and current Commercial Pilot License issued by DGCA (India).
Age - Ground Duty (Technical & Non‑Technical) 20 to 26 years as on 01 July 2027 i.e. born between 02 July 2001 and 01 July 2007 (both dates inclusive).
Marital Status Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of course; marriage is not permitted during training. Candidates marrying during training will be discharged and liable to refund the training cost.
Educational Qualification - Flying Branch Must have passed with Mathematics and Physics at 10+2 level and one of the following: - Graduation (minimum 3‑year degree) in any discipline from a recognised University; or - BE/B.Tech (4‑year degree) from a recognised University; or - Section A & B examination of AMIE (India) or Aeronautical Society of India.
Educational Qualification - Ground Duty (Technical) AE(L) Candidates must have passed Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 and have a minimum 4‑year degree in Engineering/Technology in specified Electronics/Computer/Electrical/IT disciplines or equivalent as listed in para 5.3.2.1 of the notification.
Educational Qualification - Ground Duty (Technical) AE(M) Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 with a minimum 4‑year degree in specified Mechanical/Aeronautical/Production/Industrial/Automobile/Mechatronics/Metallurgy disciplines as listed in para 5.3.2.2.
Educational Qualification - Ground Duty (Non‑Technical) - Weapon Systems (WS): Maths & Physics at 10+2 and Graduation (3‑year degree) or BE/B.Tech. - Administration & Logistics: 10+2 and Graduation in any discipline or AMIE/ASI. - Accounts: 10+2 and B.Com/BBA(BFS‑Finance)/BMS(BFS‑Finance)/BBS(BFS‑Finance)/CA/CMA/CS/CFA/B.Sc (Finance). - Education: 10+2 and Post‑Graduation in any discipline (with Graduation in any discipline). - Meteorology: 10+2 and B.Sc with Physics & Mathematics or 4‑year Engineering/Technology degree in specified streams.
Final Year Candidates Candidates in final year/semester may apply if they have no present backlog at application time and can submit proof of passing by 30 May 2027.
NCC Special Entry Mandatory NCC Air Wing Senior Division ‘C’ Certificate acquired within two years from AFCAT registration date (backward calculation). Written exam is exempted; candidates are called directly for AFSB.
GATE Score Entry Candidates with BE/B.Tech in eligible branches and valid GATE score (last 3 years from course start) in notified subjects can apply; AFCAT exam is exempted; shortlisted candidates go directly to AFSB.
Disqualifications Candidates debarred by MoD from any Defence commission, those with criminal convictions or ongoing criminal cases, and those removed from NDA/IMA/AFA/INA/OTAs for indiscipline are ineligible.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Type of Commission

Type of commission offered varies by branch and gender.

Permanent Commission (PC) Available for Men & Women; PC officers serve till superannuation in their respective branches.
Short Service Commission (SSC) - Flying Branch Engagement period of 14 years from the date of commissioning for Men and Women.
Short Service Commission (SSC) - Ground Duty (Technical & Non‑Technical) Initial tenure of 10 years with possible 4‑year extension based on service requirements, vacancies, willingness, suitability and merit.
Pension SSC officers are not entitled for pension; PC officers get pension as per rules.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Application Fee

A non‑refundable examination fee is applicable for AFCAT entry; there is no fee for NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry.

Entry Examination Fee
AFCAT Entry (All Branches incl. Tech) Rs. 550/- plus 18% GST (non‑refundable).
NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Entry No AFCAT examination fee.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Selection Process

The selection process comprises an online AFCAT exam, followed by Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) testing, medical examination and final merit list.

Stage 1 - AFCAT Online Examination

Exam Subject Duration No. of Questions Maximum Marks
AFCAT General Awareness, Verbal Ability in English, Numerical Ability, Reasoning & Military Aptitude Test. 2 Hours (120 Minutes) 100 300

Each correct answer carries 3 marks, each wrong answer attracts a penalty of 1 mark, and no marks are awarded for unattempted questions. The paper is in English only, and normalisation of marks is applied when multiple shifts are conducted.

Stage 2 - Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) Testing

  • Stage‑I: Officer Intelligence Rating Test and Picture Perception & Discussion Test on Day 1; this is a screening stage.
  • Stage‑II: Psychological tests, Group Tests and Personal Interview over the next days for Stage‑I qualified candidates.
  • CPSS: Computerised Pilot Selection System for Flying Branch candidates (once‑in‑a‑lifetime test; CPSS/PABT failures are ineligible for Flying Branch).

Stage 3 - Medical Examination & Final Merit

Candidates recommended by AFSB and found medically fit by the appropriate medical board are considered for training strictly in order of merit and branch‑wise vacancies. The final merit list is based on combined marks of AFCAT (where applicable) and AFSB.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Exam Pattern & Syllabus

The AFCAT paper is of graduation‑level standard (except Numerical Ability, which is matriculation level) and tests a mix of aptitude and knowledge.

  • English: Comprehension, error detection, sentence completion, synonyms/antonyms, cloze test, idioms & phrases, analogy, sentence rearrangement, one‑word substitution, homonyms.
  • General Awareness: History, Geography, Sports, National & International Organisations, Art & Culture, Personalities, Environment & Ecology, Polity, Economy, Basic Science, Science & Technology, Current Affairs, Defence.
  • Numerical Ability: Fractions, Time & Work, Average, Percentage, Profit & Loss, Ratio & Proportion, Interest, Time & Distance, Area & Perimeter, Probability, Number System, Mixture & Allegation, Squares/Cubes, HCF & LCM, Mensuration, Heights & Distances, Statistics.
  • Reasoning & Military Aptitude: Verbal and Non‑Verbal Reasoning.

An online practice test is available on the IAF AFCAT website.

Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 - Training, Pay & Allowances

Candidates recommended by AFSBs and found medically fit are detailed for training at Air Force Training Establishments based on merit and branch.

  • Training Date & Duration: Training is scheduled to commence in the first week of July 2027; duration is approximately 62 weeks for Flying and Ground Duty (Technical) branches and 50 weeks for Ground Duty (Non‑Technical).
  • Stipend during Training: Flight Cadets receive a fixed stipend of Rs. 56,100/- per month during one year of training (as per 7th CPC matrix).
  • Pay on Commissioning: Flying Officer - Pay Level 10 (Rs. 56,100-1,77,500) plus applicable MSP and allowances as per branch and posting.
  • Insurance: Officers are covered under Air Force Group Insurance Society (AFGIS), with Rs. 1.25 crore cover (on contribution) for death in service and Air Veterans Insurance Cover after retirement for eligible PC/SSC officers.

How to Apply for Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the AFCAT website; no other mode is accepted. Aadhaar Card is mandatory for online registration, and candidates should use valid email ID and mobile number, which must remain active throughout the selection process.

Step‑by‑Step Process (AFCAT Entry):

  1. Visit the official AFCAT website https://afcat.edcil.co.in and read the advertisement and instructions carefully.
  2. Click on the sign‑up/registration link and enter full name, mobile number and email ID, then verify via OTP on email and mobile.
  3. Agree to the declaration, submit the registration form and obtain the system‑generated User ID and Password sent to registered email and mobile.
  4. Login with User ID and Password, click on “Go to Application”, and fill personal details, communication details, qualification details, course preferences and other required fields.
  5. Upload scanned images of recent passport size colour photograph (100-200 KB), signature (80-150 KB), thumb impression (50-100 KB) and live image capture as per specified formats.
  6. Carefully verify all details using “Preview” and then proceed to pay the examination fee of Rs. 550/- + GST (for AFCAT entry only) through online payment gateway (Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card, UPI etc.).
  7. After successful payment and submission, download and print the final submitted application form for future reference.

Applicants should apply well before the last date to avoid last‑minute issues; once submitted, the application cannot be edited, and additional applications against the same Aadhaar will result in only the latest being considered.

Documents to Upload / Produce - AFCAT 02/2026

Scanned Documents to Upload (during Application):

  • Recent passport size colour photograph (100-200 KB; JPG/JPEG).
  • Signature in black ink on white paper (80-150 KB; JPG/JPEG).
  • Thumb impression - Left thumb (male) / Right thumb (female) (50-100 KB; JPG/JPEG/PDF).
  • Live image capture via device camera at the time of application.

Documents to Carry for AFCAT Exam & AFSB:

  • Printout of e‑Admit Card (AFCAT 02/2026).
  • Original Aadhaar Card and a second photo ID (PAN/Passport/Driving Licence/Voter ID etc.).
  • Two passport size colour photographs (same as uploaded).
  • Original and photocopies of Matriculation and 10+2 certificates and mark sheets.
  • Original/provisional Graduation/PG degree and mark sheets (for AFSB).
  • Certificate from College Principal for final year candidates specifying aggregate percentage and likely result date.
  • Original NCC ‘C’ Certificate (for NCC Special Entry) and GATE documents (for GATE Score Entry).
  • NOC from employer for Govt/PSU/Armed Forces personnel, if applicable.

Important Instructions for AFCAT 02/2026 Candidates

  • Candidates must ensure eligibility for the branch(es) they apply for; incorrect information at any stage leads to cancellation of candidature.
  • Use of unfair means or possession of prohibited items (mobile phones, smart watches, electronic devices, study material etc.) inside the exam hall will result in cancellation and debarment from future AFCATs.
  • No request for change of exam centre/date/slot will be entertained; centres may be changed by IAF based on administrative reasons.
  • Serving airmen must apply online like other candidates and also follow service procedures as per AFO 11/2015.
  • Use/possession of narcotics is strictly banned; candidates may be tested for drugs during medicals and training, and those found positive are liable for termination.
  • Permanent tattoos are permitted only on the inner forearms and back of hands (with conditions); non‑permissible tattoos or those with offensive/extremist content lead to rejection.
  • Training is rigorous; candidates should maintain good physical fitness (running, push‑ups, rope climbing, swimming etc.) before reporting to Air Force Academy.

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

AAI Cargo Logistics and Allied Services Company Limited (AAICLAS) has officially released Advertisement No. 01/2026 vide No. AAICLAS/HR/CHQ/Rectt./SS(F)/2026 dated 13/05/2026 for engagement of Security Screeners (Fresher) on Fixed Term Engagement Contract for a period of three years at six airport locations. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF and online application are available on the official AAICLAS website.

  • Download AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF (Advt. No. 01/2026)

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name AAI Cargo Logistics and Allied Services Company Limited (AAICLAS) – A 100% subsidiary of Airports Authority of India
Advertisement No. 01/2026 (No. AAICLAS/HR/CHQ/Rectt./SS(F)/2026) dated 13/05/2026
Post Name Security Screener (Fresher)
Total Vacancies 158 (across 6 airport locations)

Stipend during Training Rs. 15,000/- per month (stipend period); reduced to Rs. 10,000/- per month on failure in 1st attempt of certification examinations
Consolidated Remuneration (After Certification) 1st Year: Rs. 30,000/- p.m. | 2nd Year: Rs. 32,000/- p.m. | 3rd Year: Rs. 34,000/- p.m. (inclusive of all)
Application Mode Online only (via AAICLAS website www.aaiclas.aero under "Career" column)

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 158 tentative vacancies have been announced for the post of Security Screener (Fresher) across 6 airport locations. Candidates can apply for a single location only. The place of posting can be changed to any other location within India at the sole discretion of AAICLAS Management. The indicated number of vacancies are tentative and may increase or decrease as per operational requirements.

Sl. No. Airport / Place of Posting No. of Vacancies
1 Dehradun 17
2 Kolkata 25
3 Patna 37

6 Udaipur 34

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidate must be a citizen of India. Candidates not willing to serve on All India Basis need not apply. Candidates who had been selected under previous AAICLAS advertisements in the last 3 years from the date of this advertisement and were terminated due to unsuccessful completion or failure in trainings/examinations need not apply. The eligibility criteria are as on 01-04-2026 (qualification) and 01-05-2026 (age).

While filling up the online form, candidates must enter the percentage of marks of Graduation in numeric form only (e.g. 60, 70, 80 etc.) and not in CGPA grade or any other format.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Essential Qualification Graduation from any recognized Board/University/Institution with minimum 60% marks for General/OBC/EWS candidates and minimum 55% marks for SC/ST candidates
Language Requirement Ability to read/speak English, Hindi and/or conversant with local language
Medical Fitness Requirements Eye/Color blindness examination; unimpaired vision and hearing ability; ability to recognize objects highlighted by X-ray equipment; good oral and written communication skills; good physical strength and ability

Job Profile

The selected candidates will carry out Screening of Cargo & In-line Hold Baggage by technical or other means, which includes identifying and/or carrying out other Security functions at Airport & Cargo Complexes.

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limit is calculated as on 01-05-2026. The date of birth as recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination certificates will only be accepted. No subsequent requests for change in date of birth will be entertained.

Category Upper Age Limit
General / OBC / EWS 27 years (as on 01-05-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (max 32 years)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 30 years)
Ex-Serviceman 5 years relaxation (max 32 years)

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee online through net banking/credit cards/debit cards/UPI/Wallet etc. Bank charges/commission applicable for the selected mode of payment shall be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstances.

Category Application Fee
General and OBC Category Rs. 750/- (excluding Bank Charges & Other Taxes)
SC / ST / EWS & Women Rs. 100/- (excluding Bank Charges & Other Taxes)

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Pay, Allowances & Training Details

All provisionally selected and joined candidates will initially be given a stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month. After successful passing of all requisite training examinations (fee and TA/DA for 1st attempt paid by AAICLAS), the stipend will be converted to full monthly remuneration. The contract of three years shall start after passing of all certification examinations and not from the date of joining on stipend.

In case a candidate fails in the 1st attempt, the monthly stipend is reduced to Rs. 10,000/- per month and training costs for the 2nd attempt must be borne by the candidate. If the candidate fails in the 2nd attempt, the Fixed Term Contract shall be terminated without further notice.

Consolidated Remuneration (After Certification as Security Screener – Certified)

Period Emoluments (Inclusive of All)
First Year Rs. 30,000/- Fixed Per Month
Second Year Rs. 32,000/- Fixed Per Month
Third Year Rs. 34,000/- Fixed Per Month

Additional Benefits

TA/DA/Lodging & Boarding (on tour) Equivalent to three tier AC rail fare
Leave Privilege Leave – 18 per year + 12 Half Pay Leave (sick leave) + 9 CL + 2 RH
PF Contribution Equivalent to ceiling wage of Rs. 15,000/- i.e., Rs. 1,800/- p.m. as per prevailing EPF Act
Medical Insurance Maximum Rs. 10,000/- per annum reimbursed on purchase of medical insurance for self & dependent family members (on production of certified tax invoice/receipt, if not getting medical benefits from any other source)
Gratuity As per Gratuity Act, wherever applicable

Training Schedule after Joining (All arranged by AAICLAS for 1st Attempt)

Sl. No. Training / OJT Tentative Duration Remarks
01 AVSEC Induction Course 05 days Conducted at BCAS approved ASTIs of Airport Operator / Airline
02 At Airport / RA 03 Months Complete 3 months in civil aviation security operations before appearing for BASIC AVSEC
03 AVSEC BASIC Course 14 days Conducted at BCAS approved ASTIs of Airport Operator / Airline
04 At Airport (OJT on Security Equipment) 01 Month Complete minimum 40 hours OJT on Security Equipment under guidance
05 Screener's Pre-Certification Course (RA or ILHBS) 03 days Conducted at approved ASTI of Airport Operator
06 Testing & Certification of Screener – Standalone (RA or ILHBS) 02 days Conducted by BCAS

AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Candidates with higher percentage of marks at Graduation level will be called first for online interaction in a phased manner, subject to availability of vacancies at a particular station/location. Selection will be done according to the merit list prepared on the basis of marks secured in the interview. Merely appearing in the interview does not reserve any right for selection.

The date of interaction will be intimated to candidates on their registered email IDs and by uploading a circular on the AAICLAS website www.aaiclas.aero. No TA/DA or reimbursement shall be made by AAICLAS for the selection process. After selection, candidates must provide a surety bond of Rs. 1,50,000/- on non-judicial stamp paper of Rs. 100/- (duly notarized) for non-completion of the 3-year engagement period.

Stage Details
Stage 1 Online Application submission at www.aaiclas.aero under "Career" column against Advt. No. 01/2026 on or before 08-06-2026
Stage 2 Shortlisting based on Graduation percentage (higher percentage called first in phased manner)
Stage 3 Online Interaction / Interview (date and mode intimated via registered email ID and AAICLAS website)
Stage 4 Medical Fitness Test (including eye/color blindness examination, vision and hearing, object recognition ability)
Stage 5 Final engagement on Fixed Term Contract basis; surety bond of Rs. 1,50,000/- to be submitted by selected candidates

How to Apply for AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the AAICLAS official website www.aaiclas.aero by clicking on the "Apply Now" tab against Advertisement No. 01 of 2026 under the "Career" column. No application will be accepted after 08-06-2026. Applications received through Speed Post, Registered Post, Ordinary Post or Couriers will not be accepted. Only online applications will be accepted.

Candidates can apply for a single location only. Only a single application will be accepted from each candidate. The email ID entered in the application form must remain active until the engagement process is completed; no change in email ID will be allowed once entered. All communications by AAICLAS regarding this recruitment will be made only on the registered email ID of the candidates and not by post.

While filling the online form, percentage of marks of Graduation must be entered in numeric form only (e.g. 60, 70, 80 etc.) and not in CGPA grade or any other format. Candidates already working in any Government Department/PSU/Autonomous Body must produce an NOC from their present employer.

Documents to be Attached with the Online Application Form:

  • Matriculation / Higher Secondary Certificates
  • Graduation Certificate / Degree or Provisional Degree Certificate
  • Graduation Mark-sheet
  • Caste / Category Certificate (if applicable)
  • Aadhaar Card copy
  • One recent passport size coloured photograph (max 20KB size)
  • Application Fee payment proof (online)
  • Scanned Signature (max 20KB size)

*At the time of joining (if selected), candidates must bring all original certificates/mark sheets/experience certificates/professional qualification certificates/training certificates/Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Police Clearance Certificate (PCC)/Category Certificate (if applicable)/other documents along with one set of self-attested copies.

Important Instructions for AAICLAS Security Screener Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates not willing to serve on All India Basis need not apply.
  • Only a single application per candidate will be accepted and candidates can apply for a single location only.
  • Candidates selected under previous AAICLAS advertisements in the last 3 years and terminated due to failure in trainings/examinations need not apply.
  • Percentage of marks at Graduation must be entered in numeric form only; CGPA or any other format will not be accepted.
  • All communications from AAICLAS will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates must keep checking their registered email regularly.
  • No change in email ID will be allowed once entered in the application form.
  • After selection, candidates must submit a surety bond of Rs. 1,50,000/- on non-judicial stamp paper of Rs. 100/- (duly notarized) for non-completion of the 3-year engagement period.
  • The contract period of 3 years starts after passing all certification examinations and not from the date of joining on stipend.
  • During stipend period only one casual leave per month shall be applicable on pro-rata basis.
  • Maximum two attempts will be given for qualifying certification examinations; failure in both attempts leads to termination of Fixed Term Contract without further notice.
  • Training costs for the 2nd attempt (if applicable) must be borne by the candidate; AAICLAS will only make the nomination.
  • Notice period for resignation is minimum 3 months; those resigning with immediate effect must deposit 3 months' salary and no experience letter/verification will be issued.
  • Candidates working in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must produce a NOC from their present employer.
  • Furnishing false/incorrect information or suppression of material facts at any stage will result in cancellation of candidature or termination of engagement.
  • No TA/DA or reimbursement shall be made by AAICLAS for the selection process.
  • The job is transferable to any station due to operational requirements; place of posting can be changed to any location within India at sole discretion of AAICLAS Management.
  • Any canvassing or bringing political/outside influence shall be treated as disqualification.
  • All further announcements/details pertaining to this recruitment will be published only on www.aaiclas.aero and not by post.
  • For queries, write to [email protected] mentioning Advt. No. and position applied for, or call helpdesk at 011-24667781.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BOB recruitment notification 2026 for 5000 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The online application process starts on 19th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8th June 2026. Find Bank of Baroda Apprentice vacancies across all States/UTs on the official website http://bankofbaroda.bank.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 apply online from the Bank of Baroda career page.

FOLLOW US

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda has officially released Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 5000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying. The notification PDF is available on the official Bank of Baroda website and BFSI SSC's website.

  • Download Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Bank of Baroda (BOB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Human Resource Management (HRM), Bank of Baroda
Conducted By BFSI Sector Skill Council of India (BFSI SSC)
Total Training Seats 5000 (across 35 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)
Duration of Training 12 months of On-the-Job Training
Application Mode Online only (via NATS / NAPS portals + BFSI SSC payment link)
Selection Mode Online Examination + Document Verification + Local Language Test
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Official Website https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 19/05/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 19-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application Submission 08-06-2026
Cut-Off Date for Eligibility (Age / Qualification) 01-05-2026
Online Examination Date To be notified on Bank's website and https://bfsissc.com
Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test Date To be notified after Online Examination result

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 5000 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.

Sr. No. State/UT Total
1 Andaman & Nicobar Islands 1
2 Andhra Pradesh 188
3 Arunachal Pradesh 4
4 Assam 29
5 Bihar 179
6 Chandigarh 4

Total All States/UTs 5000

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) Reservation – Categories Eligible

PwBD Category Description
Visually Impaired (VI) Blindness and Low Vision
Hearing Impaired (HI) Deaf and Hard of Hearing
Orthopedically Challenged (OC) Locomotor Disability (OA, OL, BL, OAL), Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy
Intellectual Disability (ID) Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disability and Mental Illness; & Multiple Disability
Multiple Disabilities Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Candidates must be registered on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) before applying.
Educational Qualification Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other Establishment in the past, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training with any Establishment.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
State/UT Application Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only and must select up to 3 preferred training locations/centres from those notified within that State/UT.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

Category Age Relaxation (in years)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5
Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3
PwBD – General / EWS 10
PwBD – OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13
PwBD – SC / ST 15

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

Category Total Fee / Charges
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
Person with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Male Candidates Rs. 800/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Female / Transgender Candidates Rs. 600/- plus GST

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination, Document Verification, and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

Stage 1 - Online Examination

S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
2 Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 25 25 Hindi / English
3 Computer Knowledge 25 25 Hindi / English
4 General English 25 25 English
Total 100 100  

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera, microphone, and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for, at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

Stage 3 - Test of Local Language (State-wise)

Sr. No. State / UT Language(s)

2 Andhra Pradesh Telugu / Urdu

4 Assam Assamese / Bengali / Bodo

23 Mizoram Mizo

34 Uttarakhand Hindi
35 West Bengal Bengali / Nepali

Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet having studied the specified local language of the State/UT will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates who fail to qualify the local language test will not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.

Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:

  • Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:

  • Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
  • Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
  • Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda.
  • The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid.
  • The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC.
  • No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable.
  • Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage.
  • Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination.
  • Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses.
  • Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training.
  • Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable.
  • All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

South Indian Bank Recruitment 2026 - Salary

IBA approved scale of pay for Scale I / Scale II / Scale III Officers, including Dearness Allowance, HRA, Special Allowance, and other allowances. Performance Linked Incentive as applicable to Scale I/II/III officers will be paid as per the scheme in vogue.

South Indian Bank Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on shortlisting followed by Preliminary and/or Personal Interview. Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for interview. Mere eligibility does not vest any right on the applicant to be called for the recruitment process. The Bank reserves the right to make required modifications to the selection process and to decide the number of applicants to be called, based on the volume of applications received. Shortlisting will be solely at the Bank's discretion. In all matters regarding eligibility and selection, the Bank's decision will be final and binding.

Stage Details
Shortlisting Based on eligibility, qualifications, and experience as per Bank's discretion
Preliminary and/or Personal Interview Only shortlisted candidates will be called; call letters will be sent via Email only (no hard copy)

All marks/scores/grades obtained at any stage of the selection process will be treated as strictly confidential and will not be disclosed to any party, including candidates.

How to Apply for South Indian Bank Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.southindianbank.bank.in from 15.05.2026 to 25.05.2026. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must ensure a valid email ID is available in their name before registering. There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - candidates must take utmost care while filling up the form.

Multiple registrations for the same role will lead to disqualification. On successful submission, a system-generated User ID (Application Ref. ID) will be generated and an email with application PDF and login credentials will be shared to the registered email ID. Application status can be checked by logging into the profile. Only successfully submitted applications will be considered.

Documents to be uploaded at the time of online application:

  • Photograph: Recent passport-size color photo, white background, no caps/hats/dark glasses; Resolution 378×437 pixels; max size 50 KB.
  • Signature: Signed on white paper with black ink pen; Resolution 140×110 pixels; max size 50 KB.
  • Resume: PDF format; max size 1 MB.

Physical copy of the application need not be sent. Candidates are advised to visit the Careers page on the Bank's website regularly for future updates and to avoid last-minute submission due to possible website congestion near the closing date.

Apply Online at: www.southindianbank.bank.in → About Us → Careers

For queries contact: Toll Free: 0484 2771383 / 1800-425-1809 / 1800-102-9408 | Email: [email protected]

Important Instructions for South Indian Bank Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Beware of fraudulent job alerts - apply only through the official online portal; all communication will be via official channels only.
  • Candidates must ensure they fulfil all eligibility criteria (age, qualification, experience) before applying.
  • Information furnished in the online application will be treated as final; if found false at a later date, selection/appointment will be liable for termination.
  • SC/ST candidates must submit attested copies of certificates from a competent authority to prove their status as and when called.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Candidates must appear for the recruitment process at their own cost.
  • Only candidates willing to serve anywhere in India should apply.
  • Appointment is subject to medical fitness, satisfactory background verification including CIBIL/credit history check, police clearance check, and completion of other formalities as per Bank rules.
  • Shortlisting is provisional and without verification of original documents; candidature is subject to document verification when called for interview/training/joining.
  • Call letters will be sent via email only - no hard copy will be sent.
  • All revisions/corrigendum will be hosted on the Bank's website only.
  • Candidates must retain two copies of the photograph used in the application for use at the time of recruitment process.
  • The Bank does not assume responsibility for inability to submit application due to internet unavailability or any reason beyond its control.
